speaker: Computerized Voice
text: Simulation terminated. Simulation terminated. Simulation terminated. Simulation terminated. Simulation terminated.
parenthetical: (Work lights flash on, flooding the room with light. The boy, now revealed to be an animatronic, winds down and resets. One wall of the bedroom starts to rise like a garage door opening, revealing the bedroom to be just a set, a simulator where monsters practice their scare tactics. Behind a control console sits an evaluator, the dragon/lizard hybrid-like Ms. Flint. Judging by her tone, she's been through this before.)

speaker: Ms. Flint
text: Alright, Mr. Bile, is it?

speaker: Bile
text: Uh, my friends call me Phlegm.

speaker: Ms. Flint
text: Uh-huh. Mr. Bile, can you tell me what you did wrong?

speaker: Bile
text: I fell down?

speaker: Ms. Flint
text: No, no, before that. Can anyone tell me Mr. Bile's big mistake? Anyone? Pupil 1:
parenthetical: (Next point of views shows three pathetic-looking monsters taking notes. Judging from their looks, they don't seem to know much about proper scaring.)

speaker: Ms. Flint
text: Let's take a look at the tape. Here we go.
parenthetical: (Ms. Flint rewinds the tape. Bile enters the bedroom, leaving the door wide open.)

speaker: Ms. Flint
text: Right... p-p-p-p-p- Ah! See? The door! You left it wide open.

speaker: Pupils and Bile
text: Oooh.

speaker: Ms. Flint
text: And leaving a door open is the worst mistake any employee can make because...?

speaker: Bile
text: Um... it could... let in a draft?
parenthetical: (Suddenly a sharp, strict voice bellows)

speaker: Waternoose
text: It could let in a child!

speaker: Ms. Flint
text: Oh! Mr. Waternoose!
parenthetical: (Pupils gasp with surprise)

speaker: Waternoose
text: There's nothing more toxic or deadly than a human child. A single touch could KILL YOU!
parenthetical: (Bile steps away from the animatronic child nervously upon hearing that.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: Leave a door open, and a child could walk right into this factory! Right into the monster world! Pupil 2: I won't go in a kid's room! You can't make me!

speaker: Waternoose
text: You're going in there! Because we need this!
parenthetical: (Waternoose unlocks the armor can, and the sound of children's screams leak out. The lights surge, and monitors crackle. The pupils cover their ears. Waternoose corks the armor can. The electricity stops surging.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: Our city is counting on you to collect those children's screams. Without scream, we have no power. Yes, it's dangerous work, and that's why I need you to be at your best. I need scarers who are confident, tenacious, tough, intimidating. I need scarers like... like... James P. Sullivan. Advertisement

speaker: Mike
text: Hey, good morning, Monstropolis. It's now five after the hour of 6:00 A.M. in the big monster city. Temperature's a balmy 65 degrees, which is good news for you reptiles, and it looks like it's gonna be a perfect day to maybe, hey, just lie in bed, sleep in, or simply... ...WORK OUT THAT FLAB THAT'S HANGIN' OVER THE BED! GET UP, SULLEY!
parenthetical: [imitating Sulley's radio]

speaker: Sulley
text: AHH! AHH! AAAHH!
parenthetical: (In the living room, Sulley drops to the floor for push-ups. Mike stands in front of him barking orders like a coach.)

speaker: Sulley
text: I don't believe I ordered a wake-up call, Mikey.

speaker: Mike
text: Hey, less talk, more pain, marshmallow boy! Feel the burn!
parenthetical: (Between each push-up, Sulley springs into the air, striking a fearsome pose and roaring.)

speaker: Mike
text: You call yourself a monster?
parenthetical: (Sulley roars louder. Later, Sulley jogs in place.)

speaker: Mike
text: Scary feet, scary feet, scary feet-- Oop! The kid's awake!
parenthetical: (Sulley drops to the ground and lies motionless, then jogs again.)

speaker: Mike
text: Okay, scary feet, scary feet, scary feet, scary feet, scar-- Kid's asleep!
parenthetical: (Sulley roars.)

speaker: Mike
text: Twins! In a bunk bed!
parenthetical: (Sulley goes up and down, roaring on two levels.)

speaker: Mike
text: Ooh, I thought I had ya there.
parenthetical: (Later, Mike holds a broom with a crude drawing of a child's face taped to the end.)

speaker: Mike
text: Okay, Sulley, here we go. You ready? Follow it!
parenthetical: (Mike swings the broom, Sulley lunges after it.)

speaker: Mike
text: Oh! It's over here! Oh! Look over there! Don't let the kid touch you! Don't let it touch you!
parenthetical: (Sulley growls at the paper kid while dodging it. Later, like a quarterback rushing a tackle dummy, Sulley strains to push a pile of heavy furniture across the living room. Mike stands atop the pile encouraging him.)

speaker: Mike
text: I don't know, but it's been said. I love scaring kids in bed!
parenthetical: (Later in the bathroom, Sulley brushes his teeth, while Mike stands on his arm.)

speaker: Mike
text: C'mon, fight that plaque! Fight that plaque! Scary monsters don't have plaque!
parenthetical: (Later in the living room, Sulley hangs upside-down from a beam, doing some "gravity" sit-ups.)

speaker: Mike
text: One-eighteen. Do you have one-nineteen? Do I see one-twenty? Oh, I don't believe it!

speaker: Sulley
text: I'm not even breakin' a sweat.

speaker: Mike
text: Not you. Look! The new commercial's on.
parenthetical: (Mike runs to the TV, Sulley slips and falls to the floor. On the TV, a bank of lights illuminate and flare brightly.)

speaker: Announcer
text: The future is bright at Monsters, Incorporated. .

speaker: Mike
text: I'm in this one. I'm in this one!
parenthetical: (On the TV, there are beautiful shots of monsters happily living their lives.)

speaker: Announcer
text: We're part of your life. We power your car. We warm your home. We light your city.
parenthetical: (Betty turns to the camera.)

speaker: Betty
text: I'm Monsters, Incorporated!
parenthetical: (Sulley watches from his easy chair.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Hey, look! Betty!
parenthetical: (On the TV, a bored child watches as various monsters cycle past. We settle on Claws as he roars, which causes the child to scream, which then fills up a screen canister, then goes to some energy sources from the outside of the factory, and then goes to a light switch that a monster turns on, and also does the trick for a drill, a lamp, a car, and a vacuum cleaner.)

speaker: Announcer
text: Carefully matching every child to their ideal monster, to produce superior scream, refined into clean, dependable energy. Every time you turn something on, Monsters, Incorporated is there.

speaker: Jerry
text: I'm Monsters, Incorporated!
parenthetical: (On the TV, a child is vacantly staring at a television set. Machine gun fire, explosions, etc. are heard emerging from the set; he doesn't even blink, but after a few seconds, he does blink, and then his head dunks into his bowl of cereal. Text appears on the screen: "Simulation-Not Actual Child.")

speaker: Announcer
text: We know the challenge. The window of innocence is shrinking. Human kids are harder to scare.

speaker: Waternoose
text: Of course. M.I. is prepared for the future. With the top scarers...
parenthetical: (On the screen, Sulley stands next to a door standing on a factory work floor, known as the Scare Floor. He leans next to the door, opens the door and roars. The kid inside screams. Mike watches his buddy on TV and friendly hits his arm in courage.)

speaker: Mike
text: Whoo-hoo-hoo! Ha-ha!

speaker: Waternoose
text: ...the best refineries, and research into new energy techniques.
parenthetical: (On the TV, a monster with virtual reality glasses roars, causing a computer child on a monitor behind him to scream. Mike scoots to the edge of his seat in anticipation.)

speaker: Mike
text: Okay, here I come.
parenthetical: (On the TV, Mike and Sulley stand in front of a crowd of M.I. workers. As they turn to the camera, the Monsters, Inc. logo appears over Mike, blocking him entirely.)

speaker: Sulley
text: We're working for a better tomorrow... today!
parenthetical: (Mike stares at the TV, shocked.)

speaker: Monsters
text: We're Monsters, Incorporated!

speaker: Waternoose
text: We're M.I., Monsters, Incorporated. We scare because we care.
parenthetical: (Sulley turns off the TV.)

speaker: Mike
text: I can't believe it.

speaker: Sulley
text: Oh, Mike.

speaker: Mike
text: I was on TV! Ha. Did you see me? I'm a natural!
parenthetical: (As Mike was amazed to see himself on TV, the phone rings, and Mike grabs the receiver.)

speaker: Mike
text: Hello? I know! Wasn't I great?! Did the whole family see it? It's your mom. Ha-ha, what can I say? The camera loves me. Advertisement

speaker: Mike
text: I'm tellin' you, big daddy, you're gonna be seein' this face on TV a lot more often.

speaker: Sulley
text: Yeah, like on "Monstropolis' Most Wanted"?

speaker: Mike
text: Ha, ha, ha, ha. You've been jealous of my good looks since the fourth grade, pal.
parenthetical: (They walk by a tiny monster couple.)

speaker: Tiny Monster Wife
text: Have a good day, sweetie.

speaker: Tiny Monster Husband
text: You too, hon.
parenthetical: (The tiny monster husband flies away. Later at the curbside, Mike approaches a shiny new convertible and holds up a set of keys.)

speaker: Mike
text: Whoo! OK, Sulley, hop on in.

speaker: Sulley
text: Nope. Uh-uh. Uh-uh.

speaker: Mike
text: Hey, hey, hey, hey, hey, hey, where're you goin'? What're you doin'?

speaker: Sulley
text: Mikey, there's a scream shortage. We're walking.

speaker: Mike
text: WALKING?!

speaker: Sulley
text: Yup. Come on!

speaker: Mike
text: NO, NO, NO, NO! My baby! Look! She needs to be driven! Bye, baby! I'll call you!
parenthetical: (Later at a Monstropolis street, the camera closes on a newspaper stand with headline: "ROLLING BLACKOUTS EXPECTED." Mike and Sulley walk past, Mike still complains about not driving his car today.)

speaker: Mike
text: Hey, genius, you wanna know why I bought the car?

speaker: Sulley
text: Not really.

speaker: Mike
text: To drive it! You know, like on the street, with the honk-honk, and the vroom-vroom, and no walking involved?

speaker: Sulley
text: Wa, wa, wa, wa. Give it a rest, will ya, butterball? Come on, you could use the exercise.

speaker: Mike
text: I could use the exercise? Look at you! Y-y-you have your own climate.
parenthetical: (Later, the monster kids jump rope as Mike and Sulley pass.)

speaker: Monster Kids
text: How many tentacles can jump the rope? One and two and three and four...
parenthetical: (The rope is actually one of the kid's tongues.)

speaker: Monster Kid
text: Morning, Mike! Morning, Sulley!

speaker: Sulley
text: Hey, mornin', kids. How ya doin'?

speaker: Mike
text: Hey, kids.

speaker: Tongue Monster Kid
text: Bye, Mike! Bye, Sulley!
parenthetical: (The jumping monster kid gets tangled in the tongue rope and flies into a giant eye monster watching from a window.)

speaker: Giant Eye Monster
text: Ow! Hey!
parenthetical: (Later at a Monstropolis storefront, a garbage monster sweeps garbage off the sidewalk into a dustpan. He happily tosses the contents into his mouth. A red, scaly monster, who was reading a newspaper, sneezes a great big sneeze, and fire shoots from his mouth and nose, incinerating his newspaper.)

speaker: Sneezing Monster
text: Aw, nuts.
parenthetical: (Later at a Monstropolis grocery store, Tony the Grosser arranges fruit on a stand with his many tentacles.)

speaker: Tony
text: Hey, fellas!

speaker: Mike
text: Hey, Tony! Hey, hey, hey! Tony! Pow-pow-pow-pow-pow!

speaker: Sulley
text: Tony! Bada-bing!

speaker: Tony
text: I hear somebody's close to breaking the all-time scare record.

speaker: Sulley
text: Aw, just trying to make sure there's enough scream to go around.
parenthetical: (Tony tosses Mike and Sulley two pieces of fruit.)

speaker: Tony
text: Hey! On the house.

speaker: Mike
text: Hey, thanks!

speaker: Sulley
text: Grazie!

speaker: Mike
text: Bada-bing! Follow me!
parenthetical: (Mike and Sulley pass Blobby who greets both of them with his fedora, but suddenly oozes through a grate and into the sewer below, leaving only eyes and teeth a top the grate.)

speaker: Blobby
text: Oh, great.
parenthetical: (Later at a Monstropolis crosswalk, Mike and Sulley wait to cross next to a giant monster, Ted, who looks like Rex from Toy Story. Because of his height, we only see Ted's massive, godzilla-like legs.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Hey, Ted! Good morning!
parenthetical: (Ted clucks like a real chicken. The traffic sign changes from "DON'T STALK" to "STALK", replacing the red hand and the green monster walking. Mike, Sulley and Ted cross.)

speaker: Sulley
text: See that, Mikey? Ted's walkin' to work.

speaker: Mike
text: Big deal. Guy takes five steps and he's there. Advertisement
parenthetical: (The camera pans to the billboard advertising Monsters, Inc.)

speaker: Ricky
text: Mornin', Sulley!

speaker: Sulley
text: Mornin', Ricky!

speaker: Jerry
text: Hey, it's the Sullster!

speaker: Sulley
text: See ya on the scare floor, buddy!

speaker: Mike
text: Hey, Marge! How was jury duty?

speaker: Employee #1
text: Mornin', Sulley!
gender_rationale: grouped numbered anonymous speakers; gender not stated

speaker: Employee #2
text: How ya doin', big guy?
gender_rationale: grouped numbered anonymous speakers; gender not stated

speaker: Sulley
text: 'Ey!
parenthetical: (Twelve scarer of the month photographs -- all of Sulley -- hang on a wall. Two geeky monsters, Needleman and Smitty, straighten one of them.)

speaker: Needleman
text: Hey! It's still leaning to the left!

speaker: Smitty
text: It is not!

speaker: Sulley
text: Hey, fellas! Hey, Jerry!

speaker: Jerry
text: Morning!
parenthetical: (The geeks hear Sulley's voice and run up to him.)

speaker: Needleman
text: Hey, Mr. Sullivan!

speaker: Sulley
text: Guys, I told you, call me Sulley.
parenthetical: (The geeks laugh like schoolgirls.)

speaker: Smitty
text: I don't think so.

speaker: Needleman
text: We just wanted to wish you good luck today.

speaker: Mike
text: Hey, hey, hey, hey, hey, hey. Come on, get lost, you two. You're makin' him lose his focus.

speaker: Needleman
text: Oh, sorry.

speaker: Sulley
text: See you later, fellas.
parenthetical: (Sulley heads off with Mike.)

speaker: Smitty
text: Go get 'em, Mr. Sullivan!

speaker: Needleman
text: Quiet! You'll make him lose his focus.

speaker: Smitty
text: Oh, no. Sorry!

speaker: Needleman
text: Shut up!
parenthetical: (At the reception desk, Celia Mae is answering phone calls.)

speaker: Celia
text: Monsters, Inc., please hold. Monsters, Inc., I'll connect you. Ms. Fearmonger is on vacation, would you like her voicemail?
parenthetical: (Mike and Sulley approach the desk.)

speaker: Mike
text: Oh, Schmoopsie-Poo!
parenthetical: (Celia turns with a big, bright smile. So do a collection of snakes, which we now realize make up Celia's hair.)

speaker: Celia
text: Googly Bear!
parenthetical: (Her snakes sigh contentedly.)

speaker: Mike
text: Happy birthday.

speaker: Celia
text: Oh, Googly-Woogly, you remembered!
parenthetical: (Celia leans forward and gently rubs Mike's head. They're nauseatingly cute together.)

speaker: Celia
text: Hey, Sulley-Wulley.

speaker: Sulley
text: Oh, hey, Celia... Welia. Happy birthday.

speaker: Celia
text: Thanks. So, uh, are we going anywhere special tonight?

speaker: Mike
text: I just got us into a little place called, um... "Harryhausen's."

speaker: Celia
text: Harryhausen's?! But it's impossible to get a reservation there.

speaker: Mike
text: Not for Googly Bear.
parenthetical: (Celia giggles. So do the snakes.)

speaker: Mike
text: I will see you at quittin' time and not a minute later.

speaker: Celia
text: OK, sweetheart.

speaker: Mike
text: Think romantical thoughts. You and me, me and you, both of us together!

speaker: Mike
text: You know, pal, she's the one. That's it. She is the one!

speaker: Sulley
text: I'm happy for you.

speaker: Mike
text: Oh, and, uh, thanks for hookin' me up with those reservations.
parenthetical: (Mike puts the giant bowl -- his contact lens -- into his eye.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Oh, no problem. They're under the name "Googly Bear."

speaker: Mike
text: Oh, good id- You know, that wasn't very funny.
parenthetical: (Mike's locker suddenly slams by itself. Confused, Mike opens it. It slams shut again.)

speaker: Mike
text: What the...?
parenthetical: (A purple, mad scientist/creepy-looking chameleon/anaconda hybrid-like monster named Randall Boggs, materializes and jumps out at Mike.)

speaker: Randall
text: WAZOWSKI!

speaker: Mike
text: AHHHHHH!!
parenthetical: (Mike flies backwards, tripping over the bench.)

speaker: Randall
text: What do ya know? It scares little kids and little monsters.

speaker: Mike
text: I wasn't scared, I have... allergies.

speaker: Randall
text: Uh-huh, sure.
parenthetical: [unconvincingly]

speaker: Sulley
text: Hey, Randall. Save it for the scare floor, will ya?
parenthetical: (Randall performs a series of martial art moves.)

speaker: Randall
text: I'm in the zone today, Sullivan. Gonna be doing some serious scaring, putting up some big numbers.

speaker: Mike
text: Wow, Randall, that's great. That should make it even more humiliating when we break the record first. Ha-ha!

speaker: Randall
text: Shh, shh, shh, shh, shh. Do you hear that?
parenthetical: (Randall pauses for a dramatic effect.)

speaker: Randall
text: It's the winds of change.
parenthetical: (Randall exits. Mike watches him go, smoldering.)

speaker: Mike
text: "You hear it? You hear the winds of cha--?" What a creep. One of these days, I am really... gonna let you teach that guy a lesson.

speaker: Mike
text: Chalooby. Baby.
parenthetical: (Roz is reading a newspaper.)

speaker: Mike
text: Good morning, Roz, my succulent little garden snail. And who will we be scaring today?
parenthetical: (Mike reaches for paperwork on the desk, but Roz's wet hand lands on it first. She's a slug-like monster with horn-rimmed glasses.)

speaker: Roz
text: Wazowski! You didn't file your paperwork last night.

speaker: Mike
text: Oh, that darn paperwork! Wouldn't it be easier if it all just blew away?
parenthetical: (Roz leans forward in her chair threateningly.)

speaker: Roz
text: Don't let it happen again.

speaker: Mike
text: Yes, well, uh, I'll try to be less careless.
parenthetical: (Mike grabs the paperwork and shuffles away.)

speaker: Roz
text: I'm watching you, Wazowski. Alllways watching. Always.

speaker: Mike
text: Ooh, she's nuts.
parenthetical: (Mike walks onto the Scare Floor, already bustling with activity)

speaker: Celia
text: All scare floors are now active. Assistants, please report to your stations.
parenthetical: (Mike fits an empty can onto his door station. Mike swipes a cardkey from a child's folder and slides it through a slot on the station. A child's closet door rides out into the Scare Floor on an overhead track. Several assistants follow suit. Doors ride out of the vault and land in stations. The shutters close on the factory windows. A Jumbotron screen displaying the M.I. logo switches over to a map. The eastern portion of the United States is illuminated as Jerry steps up.)

speaker: Jerry
text: OK, people, Eastern Seaboard comin' online. We got scarers comin' out!
parenthetical: (The assistants run to their stations and stand at attention. The scarers stride onto the Scare Floor in slow motion. These are the best of the best; the top Scarers, ready for action. Smitty and Needleman, the two geeky teenage janitors stand in awe on the sidelines.)

speaker: Needleman
text: Oh, they're so awesome!
parenthetical: (The Scarers stop opposite their doors. Their assistants approach them and make last minute preparations. Sulley cracks his knuckles and shakes to keep it loose. Pete "Claws" Ward flips out his retractable claws like switchblades and roars. Bob "Dentures" Peterson is handed a set of false large teeth. He slides the chompers into place and snarls. Randall practices camouflaging by blending into various background patterns: wood, brick, wallpaper. His nerdy assistant Fungus alternates the patterns. Augustus "Spike" Jones puts a tentacle in his mouth and blows. Spikes pop out of his skin like a porcupine and growls. Ricky Plesuski opens his mouth to reveal a giant set of spiked teeth. His assistant brushes them. Ted Pauley grabs a handful of eyeballs from his assistant and squishes them into his face. One of the eyeballs falls off Ted's face, and his assistant puts it back on. The Jumbotron now shows everyone's scare totals. Sulley is at the top, with Randall in second place.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Hey, may the best monster win.

speaker: Randall
text: I plan to.
parenthetical: (Jerry begins the countdown.)

speaker: Jerry
text: We are on in seven, six,
parenthetical: (Mike hits a button on his keypad. The red light above his door lights up. Other assistants do the same.)

speaker: Jerry
text: five, four, three, two...
parenthetical: (Jerry silently indicates "one" with his hand. A sign light above the Scare Floor changes from "STANDBY" to "SCARE", replacing the red monster to a green monster. The scaring begins. A horn sounds, and each scarer rushes up to their door. Sulley disappears through the door.)

speaker: Mike
text: You're the boss, you're the boss, you're the big hairy boss.
parenthetical: (Randall and the other monsters follow suit. Screams are heard from Sulley's inside door. Sulley exits and checks his new scare totals on the jumbo-tron.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Oh, I'm feelin' good today, Mikey!
parenthetical: (Sulley's tally goes up significantly.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Yeah!

speaker: Mike
text: Whoa! Attaboy. Attaboy. Another door comin' right up.
parenthetical: (A scream comes from another door. Randall emerges to watch his tally go up, but only slightly. Randall growls in anger, as his assistant Fungus scratches his head.)

speaker: Fungus
text: You're still behind, Randall. You know, maybe I should realign the scream intake valve...?

speaker: Randall
text: JUST GET ME ANOTHER DOOR!

speaker: Fungus
text: Aah! A door! Yes! Door!
parenthetical: (The monsters work fast and furiously. Children's screams are heard everywhere, filling the cans. Waternoose walks up beside Jerry.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: Well, Jerry, what's the damage so far?

speaker: Jerry
text: We may actually make our quota today, sir.

speaker: Waternoose
text: Hmm. First time in a month.
parenthetical: (A scream can gurgles as its valve staggers and drops, registering empty. Rock music pounds as the door opens and Ward scrambles out of the room, sobbing pathetically.)

speaker: Ward's assistant
text: What happened?

speaker: Ward
text: The kid almost touched me! She got this close to me!

speaker: Ward's assistant
text: She wasn't scared of you? She was only six!

speaker: Ward
text: I COULD'VE BEEN DEAD!! I COULD HAVE DIED!
parenthetical: (Ward's assistant slaps him across the face.)

speaker: Ward's assistant
text: Keep it together, man! Hey, we got a dead door over here!
parenthetical: (Needleman and Smitty come running, wheeling a portable door shredder.)

speaker: Needleman
text: We're coming! Look out!

speaker: Smitty
text: Out of the way! Excuse us.

speaker: Needleman
text: Coming through.
parenthetical: (Needleman tapes a yellow "X" across the door.)

speaker: Jerry
text: We've lost fifty-eight doors this week, sir.

speaker: Waternoose
text: Oh, kids these days. They just don't get scared like they used to.
parenthetical: (Needleman and Smitty load the defunct door into the shredder.)

speaker: Needleman
text: Let 'er rip!
parenthetical: (The door shreds with a buzz, spewing out sawdust and leaving only two doorknobs. An octopus-like monster named Josh Rivera runs toward a door with his tentacles flying.)

speaker: Josh Rivera
text: *Ululating*
parenthetical: (Randall emerges from another door. He's working hard.)

speaker: Fungus
text: Uh, sir?

speaker: Randall
text: WHAT?!

speaker: Fungus
text: L-look!
parenthetical: (Fungus points to the tally board. Randall's name replaces Sulley's as number one.)

speaker: Celia
text: Attention. We have a new scare leader, Randall Boggs. Charlie, Waxford and Frank: Nice job! You took the lead! You did it! Look at those numbers!
parenthetical: (Charlie, Waxford and Frank gather around Randall, congratulating him.) | (Multiple screams draw the attention of the admirers. In seconds, Mike deftly fills can after can with scream. Sulley emerges from the door and cracks his knuckles.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Slumber party.
parenthetical: (The tally board updates; Sulley's back in first place.)

speaker: Celia
text: Never mind. Charlie, Waxford and Frank: What?!
parenthetical: (Charlie, Waxford and Frank push past Randall as they rush to Sulley.)

speaker: Randall
text: Hey, watch it! Charlie, Waxford and Frank: That was awesome! How did you do that? You're going to the hall of fame for sure!
parenthetical: (Sulley gives his fans a high five as Waternoose approaches.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: Well, James, that was an impressive display.

speaker: Sulley
text: Oh, just doin' my job, Mr. Waternoose. Of course, I did learn from the best.
parenthetical: (Waternoose and Sulley both chuckle. Randall watches angrily. Fungus stands next to him nervously.)

speaker: Randall
text: If I don't see a new door in my station in five seconds , I WILL PERSONALLY PUT YOU THROUGH THE SHREDDER!!!

speaker: Fungus
text: Aaaaah! Advertisement

speaker: Charlie
text: Hey, Wazowski, nice job. Those numbers are pretty sweet.

speaker: Mike
text: Are they? You know, I hadn't even noticed. And, uh, how's Georgie doin'?

speaker: Charlie
text: He's doin' great! I love workin' with that big guy.
parenthetical: (Charlie's door opens, and a goofy-looking furry monster named George Sanderson emerges.)

speaker: George
text: Keep the doors comin', Charlie. I'm on a roll today.

speaker: Charlie
text: George and I are like brothers. 23-19!! WE HAVE A 23-19!!!!
parenthetical: (Jerry gasps and runs over to hit a human child emergency button. A siren blares. The window shutters open quickly, and the camera pans to the Jumbo-tron, which reads "WARNING: CONTAMINATION ALERT", replacing the yellow circle with a black child inside.)

speaker: Computer voice
text: Red alert! Red alert! Red alert! Red alert! Red alert!
parenthetical: (George looks around, confused. On the Jumbotron behind him is a security cam view of his back, showing the sock.)

speaker: Computer voice
text: George Sanderson, please remain motionless. Prepare for decontamination.
parenthetical: (George, realizing he caused the alarms, tries frantically to remove the sock.)

parenthetical: (Yellow-suited figures known as the CDA rappel through the windows from the ceiling overhead.)

gender_rationale: grouped numbered CDA agents (CDA agent #N); individual agents not distinguished, gender not stated

text: All clear. Situation is niner-niner-zero. Ready for de-con.

text: Hey, thanks, guys, that was a close one.

speaker: CDA agent #2
text: Okay.

A small shower head pops up, spraying disinfectant. The curtain flies open. George is entirely shaved, wearing an Elizabethan dog collar. A band-aid covers the area "contaminated" by the sock. An agent takes off the band-aid.)
gender_rationale: grouped numbered CDA agents (CDA agent #N); individual agents not distinguished, gender not stated

speaker: George
text: AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAGH!!!
parenthetical: (A sign on the wall reading "DAYS SINCE LAST ACCIDENT" flips from 47 to 0.)

speaker: Jerry
text: Okay, people, take a break! We gotta shut down for a half-hour and reset the system.
parenthetical: (Waternoose and Sulley watch as CDA agents file off the scare floor.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: An entire scare floor out of commission. What else could go wrong?
parenthetical: (Waternoose and Sulley approach the “coffee" machine. Waternoose pours himself a cup of the thick gooey liquid.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: Oh, what a day.

speaker: Sulley
text: We're just going through a rough time, sir. Everyone knows you're gonna get us through it.

speaker: Waternoose
text: Tell that to the board of directors. James, this company has been in my family for three generations. I would do anything to keep it from going under.

speaker: Sulley
text: So would I, sir.
parenthetical: (Waternoose is suddenly struck with an idea.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: Say, I could use your help with something.

speaker: Sulley
text: Anything, sir.

speaker: Waternoose
text: You see, we hired some new scare recruits, and frankly they're... They're... Um... Uh...

speaker: Sulley
text: Inexperienced?

speaker: Waternoose
text: Well, they stink.

speaker: Sulley
text: Uh-huh.

speaker: Waternoose
text: I thought maybe you might come by tomorrow and give them a demonstration, show them what it takes to be a top scarer. Huh?

speaker: Sulley
text: I'll start out with the old Waternoose Jump-and-Growl.
parenthetical: (Sulley demonstrates the "old Waternoose jump and growl". Waternoose is so startled that he drops his coffee cup.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: Aaah! Ha-ha! Oh, yes. Now, that's my boy.
parenthetical: (Waternoose pats Sulley on the shoulder. They laugh.)

text: Let's go, everybody. All doors must be returned. No exceptions!
parenthetical: (All doors are ejected and return to the vault.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Another day like this, and that scare record's in the bag!
parenthetical: [Signs the paperwork and throws it on the desk]

text: That's right, baby!

speaker: Sulley
text: Uh-huh.
parenthetical: (The overhead lights turn off as everyone leaves for the day. Mike and Sulley are at their lockers.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Nice.

speaker: Mike
text: What's on your agenda?

speaker: Sulley
text: I'm gonna head home and work out some more.

speaker: Mike
text: Again? You know, there's more to life than scaring.
parenthetical: (Mike sniffs under his arm and cringes.)

speaker: Mike
text: Whew! Hey, can I borrow your odorant?

speaker: Sulley
text: Yeah, I got, uh, Smelly Garbage, or Old Dumpster.

speaker: Mike
text: You got, uh, Low Tide?

speaker: Sulley
text: No.

speaker: Mike
text: How about Wet Dog?

speaker: Sulley
text: Yup. Stink it up.
parenthetical: (Sulley tosses Mike the can of "odorant". Mike and Sulley walk down a crowded hallway.)

speaker: Mike
text: You know, I am so romantic, sometimes I think I should just marry myself!

speaker: Sulley
text: Give me a break, Mike...

speaker: Mike
text: What a night of romance I got ahead of me! Tonight is about me, and...
parenthetical: (Mike spots Celia tidying up her desk.)

speaker: Mike
text: Celia! Ooh, the love boat is about to set sail! 'Cause I gotta tell ya, buddy, that face of hers, it just makes my heart go...
parenthetical: (As Mike turns back to Celia, he is surprised to instead see Roz.)

speaker: Mike
text: YIKES!!!

speaker: Roz
text: Hello, Wazowski. Fun filled evening planned for tonight?

speaker: Mike
text: Well, as a matter of fact--

speaker: Roz
text: And I'm sure you filed your paperwork correctly... for once.
parenthetical: (Mike freezes.)

speaker: Roz
text: Your stunned silence is very reassuring.
parenthetical: (Roz slithers away.)

speaker: Mike
text: Oh, no, my scare reports! I left them on my desk! If I'm not at the restaurant in five minutes, they're gonna give our table away; what am I gonna tell--?!
parenthetical: (Celia leaps in and squeezes Mike's cheek.)

speaker: Mike
text: Schmoopsie-Poo.

speaker: Celia
text: Hey, Googly Bear, wanna get going?

speaker: Mike
text: Uh, do I ever. It's just that... Uh, you know it's...

speaker: Celia
text: What?

speaker: Mike
text: There's a small...

speaker: Celia
text: I don't understand.

speaker: Mike
text: It's just that--

speaker: Sulley
text: It's just I forgot about some paperwork I was supposed to file. Mike was reminding me; thanks, buddy. Whew!

speaker: Mike
text: I was? I mean, I was! Yeah, I was.

speaker: Celia
text: Oh. Okay, let's go then.

speaker: Mike
text: We're going! On my desk, Sulley. The pink copies go to accounting, the fuchsia ones to purchasing, and the goldenrod ones go to Roz.
parenthetical: (Celia grabs Mike and pulls him away. Mike leans back.)

speaker: Mike
text: Leave the puce!

speaker: Sulley
text: So the pink copies goes to accounting, the fuchsia ones go to Roz. No, the fuchsia ones go to purchasing, and the goldenrod ones go to Roz. Man, I have no idea what puce is...
parenthetical: (Sulley picks up the reddish-brown paperwork files and shuffles through it.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Oh, that's puce.
parenthetical: (Sulley sees a lone door in a station. The door is white, and it has five pink flowers on it.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Uh, hello, anyone? There's a door here. Hmm.
parenthetical: (Sulley walks to the door. He's about to eject it when he notices that the light above the station is illuminated. It's active. Cautiously, he opens the door. Sulley peeks into the room.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Hello... Hey. Psst. Anybody scaring in here? Hello... Yo.
parenthetical: (Nothing but crickets. Sulley shrugs and shuts the door. Sulley steps away from the door, examining it curiously.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Hmm.
parenthetical: (Suddenly, there is a thump noise behind him. Sulley freezes. He turns around to see his tail being dropped by a human girl named Boo.)

speaker: Boo
text: Bucket?
parenthetical: (Sulley screams, falling over backward. Boo looks up at Sulley and smiles. Sulley tries to get away, but Boo grabs his tail. Panicked, Sulley picks her up with a pair of tongs and places her back in her room, closes the door and spins around... Only to discover that she's standing before him again. Sulley yells. Boo squeals with delight. He picks her up and carries her deep inside the room. Sulley deposits Boo on top of her bed. She giggles. Sulley runs out, but gets caught in a hanging mobile.)

speaker: Sulley
text: OH! YEOW!
parenthetical: (He falls, trips over a laundry basket, slips on a rug, and is ejected out of Boo's room. Sulley slides out of the room, covered in toys and junk from Boo's room. Suddenly, from the utility hallway, he hears a noise. Sulley scrambles to his feet and takes off. As he runs, a line of toy duckies caught on his tail quack along after him. Randall enters, wheeling a cart of scream cans towards the door. Sulley rushes to the toilets, stuffs Boo's junk into the bowl, and flushes it down.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Ahhhh...
parenthetical: (A second later it all comes back up, overflowing the toilet.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Aaah!
parenthetical: (Sulley runs to the locker room. He spots an open locker and stuffs the sopping kid stuff in. He closes the locker.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Whew.
parenthetical: (Relieved, Sulley turns and walks away. On his back, clinging to his fur, is Boo. Sulley rounds the locker bank and disappears from view. For a moment, all is quiet. One second later:)

speaker: Sulley
text: AAAAAHHHHH!!!
parenthetical: (Suddenly, Sulley runs back past the lockers, panic-stricken. Boo chases him.)

speaker: Boo
text: Kitty!
parenthetical: (Sulley cowers on a bench, backed into a corner of the room. Boo stands in front of him waving her arms. To Sulley, she is like a diseased rat.)

speaker: Sulley
text: No, no, stay back.

speaker: Boo
text: Na na na, zoo yeah out of the look.
parenthetical: (Boo is distracted, playing with a hardhat.)

speaker: Boo
text: I'm Tigger.
parenthetical: (Sulley finds an M.I. bag, scoops her up, and zips it closed. Holding the bag at arms length, he runs out of the room. Sulley runs to Boo's door, ready to toss Boo back in. As he reaches to open it, the knob rattles. Sulley's standing in the middle of the Scare Floor. He's thinking of a place to hide. Randall emerges from the door, confused.)

speaker: Randall
text: Hmmm.
parenthetical: (Randall ejects the door. As Randall walks away, the door rises, revealing Sulley hidden behind it. Boo wiggles inside the bag and makes a peep.)

speaker: Boo
text: Ahhh!
parenthetical: (Randall stops. He sneezes and continues on. As Randall exits the floor, Sulley watches as Boo's door disappears into the vault, and with it all hope of getting rid of Boo. Sulley grabs the bag and runs towards the exit to warn Mike and Celia.)

speaker: Waiters
text: Get a paper bag!
parenthetical: (Mike and Celia are seated in a romantic booth, laughing.)

speaker: Celia
text: Oh, Michael! I've had a lot of birthday - well, not a lot of birthdays, but this is the best birthday ever.
parenthetical: (Mike gazes dreamily at Celia. She grows self-conscious.)

speaker: Celia
text: What are you looking at?

speaker: Mike
text: Oh, I was just thinking about the first time I laid eye on you, how pretty you looked.

speaker: Celia
text: Stop it.

speaker: Mike
text: Your hair was shorter then.

speaker: Celia
text: Mm-hmm. I'm thinking about getting it cut.
parenthetical: (The snakes look panicked.)

speaker: Mike
text: No, no, I like it this length.
parenthetical: (The snakes sigh in relief.)

speaker: Mike
text: I like everything about you. Just the other day, someone asked me who I thought the most beautiful monster was in all of Monstropolis, and you know what I said?

speaker: Celia
text: What did you say?

speaker: Mike
text: I said...
parenthetical: (Behind Celia, Sulley presses his face up to the window with warning.)

speaker: Mike
text: Sulley?

speaker: Celia
text: Sulley?
parenthetical: (Befuddled, Mikes looks to the window. Sulley is gone.)

speaker: Mike
text: No! No, no! That's not what I was gonna say! I mean, well, sure, he's handsome, if you like the big guy, he's rugged.

speaker: Celia
text: Mike, you're not making sense.

speaker: Sushi Chefs
text: GET A PAPER BAG!
parenthetical: (Sulley steps inside the restaurant as he appears at the table and sits down.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Hi, guys! What a coincidence, running into you here!
parenthetical: (Sulley awkwardly squeezes into the booth next to Mike, setting the gym bag under the table.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Uh, I'm just gonna order somethin' to go.

speaker: Celia
text: Michael...

speaker: Mike
text: Sulley...!!!
parenthetical: (Sulley grabs a menu and whips it in front of his and Mike's faces.)

speaker: Sulley
text: I wonder what's good here. Hmm.

speaker: Mike
text: Get out of here! You're ruining everything!

speaker: Sulley
text: I went back to get your paperwork and there was a door.

speaker: Mike
text: What?!
parenthetical: (Mike peeks around the menu, grins and holds his index up awkwardly at Celia, then ducks back behind. Celia's starting to steam. Her snakes rattle in annoyance.)

speaker: Mike
text: A door?

speaker: Sulley
text: Randall was in it!

speaker: Mike
text: Wait a minute? Randall? That cheater! He's trying to boost his numbers.

speaker: Sulley
text: There's something else.

speaker: Mike
text: What?

speaker: Sulley
text: Ooklay in the ag-bay.

speaker: Mike
text: ....WHAT?!

speaker: Sulley
text: Look in the bag.
parenthetical: (Sulley motions to the floor. Mike ducks under the table, but there's nothing there.)

speaker: Mike
text: What bag?
parenthetical: (Sulley looks around frantically for the bag. Mike sees something that make his eye go wide. Sulley sees it too: The M.I. duffle bag is now giggling and hobbling away on little human kid-feet that poke out from the bottom of the bag.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Ohhh! They don't have anything I like here, so take care, Celia!
parenthetical: (Sulley charges toward the bag, stumbling into a waiter.)

speaker: Waiter
text: Excuse me, sir!

speaker: Celia
text: Michael, what's going on?

speaker: Mike
text: Celia, please try to understand!
parenthetical: (As Mike explains, he sees the bag tossed aside, revealing Boo.)

speaker: Mike
text: I have to... DO SOMETHING!!!

speaker: Celia
text: Michael?
parenthetical: (A monster photographer is about to take a picture of a happy monster couple.)

speaker: Photographer
text: On three. One, two...
parenthetical: (The posing couple sees Boo pop up over the photographer's shoulder. They scream. The photographer looks up and sees Boo.)

speaker: Photographer
text: AAAAAAHHHH!!!!!! A KID!!!
parenthetical: (The photographer's flash goes off, as Boo jumps onto the counter. Everyone in the restaurant turns towards Boo.)

speaker: Boo
text: Boo!
parenthetical: (Chaos ensues. Patrons scramble out the door, patrons screaming in horror.)

speaker: Nick Schmidt
text: A kid!
parenthetical: (The two eye worm like creatures leaps off of big legged monster and starts running, the sushi chef grabs a phone and dials.)

speaker: Sushi Chef
text: There's a kid in here! A human kid!!!
parenthetical: (Boo runs carefree through the chaos in the restaurant, exclaiming. Mike runs headlong into a pile of take-away boxes. Celia is caught up in the stampede of fleeing patrons.)

speaker: Celia
text: Googly Bear!
parenthetical: (Sulley tries to grab Boo, but she playfully evades his outstretched arms.) | (Mike dashes in with a takeaway box, which he uses to delicately scoop her up. Boo pops her head out of the take-away box and sticks out her tongue at Sulley.)

speaker: Boo
text: Pthbthbtht!
parenthetical: (Sulley hesitantly pushes her head down into the box and shuts the lid.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Come on!
parenthetical: (Sulley and Mike run out of the restaurant, leaving the M.I. gym bag behind. Then they exit as patrons scramble and still screaming.)

speaker: Mike
text: Let's get out of here!
parenthetical: (The CDA arrives in helicopters and vans.)

speaker: CDA Helicopter Pilot
text: Please remain calm. This is not a drill.

speaker: CDA agent
text: We have an 835 in progress. Please advise.
parenthetical: (Monsters run amok, screaming. Celia calls after Mike.)
gender_rationale: grouped numbered CDA agents (CDA agent #N); individual agents not distinguished, gender not stated

speaker: Celia
text: Michael? Michael!
parenthetical: (Mike spins around.)

speaker: Mike
text: Celia!
parenthetical: (A CDA Agent steps in front of Celia and pushes her away.)

speaker: CDA agent
text: Please come with me.
gender_rationale: grouped numbered CDA agents (CDA agent #N); individual agents not distinguished, gender not stated

speaker: Celia
text: Ow, stop pushing!
parenthetical: (Mike starts towards her.)

speaker: Mike
text: Hey! Get your hands off my Schmoopsie-Poo!
parenthetical: (Sulley grabs Mike and yanks him away.)

speaker: CDA agent
text: Building clear. Ready for decontamination.
parenthetical: (Sulley and Mike run down the alley.)
gender_rationale: grouped numbered CDA agents (CDA agent #N); individual agents not distinguished, gender not stated

speaker: Mike
text: Well, I don't think that date could have gone any worse! Advertisement
parenthetical: (Suddenly, a giant green plasma dome covers the restaurant, with Sulley and Mike looking back in fear. It was official: things just got worse.)

speaker: Newscaster
text: If witnesses are to be believed, there has been a child security breach for the first time in monster history.
parenthetical: (A CDA agent stands in front of the apartment buildings. A microphone is thrust in his face.)

speaker: CDA agent
text: We can neither confirm nor deny the presence of a human child here tonight.
parenthetical: (With chaos behind them, panicked Monsters speaks into camera.)
gender_rationale: grouped numbered CDA agents (CDA agent #N); individual agents not distinguished, gender not stated

speaker: Nick Schmidt
text: Well, a kid flew right over me and blasted a car with its laser vision!

speaker: Interviewee #1
text: I tried to run from it, but it picked me up with its mind powers and shook me like a doll!
parenthetical: (A monster with dozens of eyes steps forward to corroborate)
gender_rationale: grouped numbered anonymous speakers; gender not stated

speaker: Interviewee #2
text: It's true! I saw the whole thing!
parenthetical: (A scientist sits next to the anchor)
gender_rationale: grouped numbered anonymous speakers; gender not stated

speaker: Scientist
text: It is my professional opinion, that now is the time TO PANIC-!
parenthetical: (CRASH! Mike and Sulley's TV falls to the floor itself. Boo's head peeks out over the set.)

speaker: Boo
text: Oh-oh.
parenthetical: (Sulley and Mike wear colanders, snorkels, masks, and oven mitts for protection. They see the toddler and scream, scrambling behind a chair for protection.)

speaker: Mike
text: Oh, it's coming! It's coming!

speaker: Boo
text: Boo!

speaker: Sulley and Mike
text: AAAH!
parenthetical: (Sulley and Mike flee over to the window. Outside, helicopters scan the area. Hurriedly Mike and Sulley yank the shades closed. Boo totters towards them, babbling. Mike and Sulley retreat in fear.)

speaker: Sulley and Mike
text: Aaaaaaah!
parenthetical: (Boo opens the window shade, standing in full view of the helicopters outside.)

speaker: Mike
text: No, no, no, no, no, no, no, no! Come here, kid!
parenthetical: (Mike scoots her out of view using a broom, then sprays the area with disinfectant. Meanwhile, Boo says "Whee." and has discovered Mike's CD collection, neatly arranged in two stacks.)

speaker: Mike
text: No, don't touch those, you little--!
parenthetical: (The stacks come crashing down.)

speaker: Mike
text: Oh, now those were alphabetized... It's okay, it's all right. As long as it doesn't come near us, we're gonna be okay.
parenthetical: (Boo sneezes directly in Mike's eye.)

speaker: Mike
text: AAH!!
parenthetical: (Mike freaks out, accidentally spraying disinfectant squarely into his own eye!)

speaker: Mike
text: AAAAAAAAAAH!!!
parenthetical: (Now Boo approaches a scared Sulley. Whimpering, Boo says "Wanna ride on it!" and protects himself with a garbage can lid. Boo points to a one-eyed teddy bear, out of her reach.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Oh, you like this? Fetch!
parenthetical: (Sulley accidentally tosses the bear across the room. Boo runs after it. She picks up the bear and hugs it. Mike sees this and gasps. This has gone too far.)

speaker: Mike
text: Hey, hey, that's it! No one touches Little Mikey.
parenthetical: (Mike angrily grabs the bear out of Boo's hands. Stunned, her face begins to redden, and her eyes well up with tears.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Mike, give her the bear.

speaker: Mike
text: Oh, no!
parenthetical: (Boo screams at the top of her lungs as she cries, which causes the lights and electronics to surge. Sulley and Mike cover their nonexistent ears. From outside, the window and the streetlamps pulse a bright light, creating a beacon for the helicopters. Back inside, Boo wails loudly in sadness. Suddenly, the circling helicopters spot the surge and aim their spotlights on the apartment. Mike and Sulley both panic. Mike drops the bear and runs to the window, pulling the shade down.)

speaker: Mike
text: Make it stop, Sulley! MAKE IT STOP!!
parenthetical: [Boo continues to wail. Desperate, Sulley offers her the bear.]

speaker: Sulley
text: Here! See the bear? Ooh, nice bear!
parenthetical: [Boo stops crying momentarily, as the lights stabilize. It's not working. She continues to cry, while the lights start fluctuating. The helicopters are getting closer.]

speaker: Mike
text: Sulley!
parenthetical: [Desperate, Sulley begins to sing and dance with the bear; anything to get her to stop crying.]

speaker: Sulley
text: Oh, he's a happy bear, and he's not crying, and neither should you, or we'll be in trouble, 'cause they're gonna find us, so please stop crying, right now, right now...
parenthetical: [Boo stops crying as the lights in the apartment go back to normal levels. The helicopters turn around and begin to fly away.]

speaker: Mike
text: Good! Good, Sulley, keep it up, you're doin' great!

speaker: Sulley
text: Oooh, ah, the happy bear, he has no care...
parenthetical: [Boo reaches for the bear, accidentally touching Sulley's hand. He screams.]

speaker: Sulley
text: BWAAAAAAH! YAAAH! It touched me!! She touched me! AAAAGHHHH!!!!!!
parenthetical: [Sulley's yelling frightens Boo, who starts crying again! The lights in the apartment surge. Mike runs toward Sulley, reaching for the bear.]

speaker: Mike
text: Sulley, the bear! The bear! Give it the...
parenthetical: [Mike trips and flies across the floor, while the lights stop surging as Boo stops crying once again.]

speaker: Mike
text: Whoahhhhh!
parenthetical: [Mike rolls into a garbage can and bumps into a shelf, sending a stack of books into his mouth. A hanging stereo speaker lands on his head and squashes him flat. Just then, Boo thinks this is hilarious and begins laughing out loud. The lights in the apartment glow even brighter than before. From outside, the region lit up by the laugh's power radiates outwards from Mike and Sulley's window. In a moment every window on the building starts glowing brightly. Back inside the lights flare to an increasing white-hot brightness, and then POP. The laughter subsides, and all is dark.]

speaker: Sulley
text: What was that?
parenthetical: [Mike Wasowski is still wedged in the trash can.]

speaker: Mike
text: I have no idea. But it would be really great if it didn't...do it...again.

speaker: Sulley
text: Shhhh!
parenthetical: [She seems to understand. She nods and holds her tiny finger up to her mouth.]

speaker: Boo
text: Shhhh.
parenthetical: (She smiles big as she finally becomes quiet)

speaker: Sulley
text: How could I do this? How could I be so stupid? This could destroy the company.

speaker: Mike
text: The company? Who cares about the company? What about us?! That thing is a KILLING MACHINE!
parenthetical: (Boo spins around in a circle, getting dizzy)

speaker: Boo
text: La la la la la la la la...

speaker: Mike
text: I bet it's just waiting for us to fall asleep, and then, WHAM!! Oh, we're easy prey, my friend, easy prey! We're sitting targets!
parenthetical: (She falls over. Mike directs his pal to a paper on the desk.)

speaker: Mike
text: Okay, look, I think I have a plan here. Using mainly spoons, we dig a tunnel under the city and release it into the wild.
parenthetical: (Sulley stares in disbelief.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Spoons.
parenthetical: (Mike crumples up the piece of paper, and tosses it onto a large existing pile of crumpled plans.)

speaker: Mike
text: That's it. I'm out of ideas. We're closed. Hot air balloon? Too expensive. Giant slingshot? Too conspicuous. Enormous wooden horse? Too Greek!
parenthetical: (Boo holds up a drawing.)

speaker: Boo
text: Looka dooko teeko peeka!
parenthetical: (Sulley can't quite believe his eyes. The drawing is crude, but its unmistakenly a picture of Sulley and Boo holding hands.) | [gibberish]

speaker: Mike
text: No plan... no plan... can't think... can't think. Flat-lining...flat-lining...
parenthetical: (Boo yawns.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Uh, Mike, I think she's getting tired.

speaker: Mike
text: Well, then why don't you find some place for it to sleep... WHILE I THINK OF A PLAN?!
