text: How many kids you got in there?

speaker: Day-care kids
text: Mike Wazowski!
parenthetical: (Mike and Sulley turn to see a line of monster children, led by the day care worker. Boo is at the end, still in her monster costume, minus one eye stalk.)

speaker: Boo
text: Kitty!

speaker: Sulley
text: Boo!
parenthetical: (Sulley runs to Boo. He tosses the cube, which lands on Mike. Sulley picks Boo up and hugs her tightly.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Boo! Oh, you're alright. I was so worried. I was-- Don't you ever run away from me again, young lady. Oh, but I'm so glad you're safe.

speaker: Day care worker
text: My, what an affectionate father.

speaker: Sulley
text: Uh, actually, she's my, uhh, my cousin's sister's--

speaker: Mike
text: Okay, Sulley, that's enough. Let's go.
parenthetical: (A little day care kid steps up to Mike.)

speaker: Day care kid
text: Mike Wazowski!

speaker: Mike
text: Yeah, yeah, yeah, sure. Step aside, kid. We're in a--
parenthetical: (Mike waves the kid away. Without warning, the monster kid deliberately bites him.)

speaker: Mike
text: YAAAAAH!!!!
parenthetical: (Boo laughs hysterically at Mike's pain. The lights overhead glow intensely, then burst. The daycare monsters scream and cry in fright.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Will you stop makin' Boo laugh?!

speaker: Mike
text: I didn't! So come on! Advertisement
parenthetical: (Mike frees his hand. He and Sulley run down the hallway. Two of the day care kids bump into each other.)

speaker: Sulley
text: I still don't understand. You've got Boo's door?

speaker: Mike
text: I'll explain later. Run!
parenthetical: (The Scare Floor is dark and empty; everyone is still at lunch.)

speaker: Mike
text: Okay, let's move. Let's move, let's move! Come on!
parenthetical: (Mike closes his eye, not daring to hope...)

speaker: Mike
text: Oh, please be there, please be there, please be there.
parenthetical: (Mike opens his eye and spots Boo's door in a station.)

speaker: Mike
text: There it is! Just like Randall said!

speaker: Sulley
text: Randall? Wait a minute.
parenthetical: (Boo squirms out of Sulley's arms and runs off under a desk. Sulley tries to calm her.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Whoa, whoa, hey!

speaker: Mike
text: 1, 2, 3, 4! Get the kid back through the door!

speaker: Sulley
text: Hey, hey.

speaker: Mike
text: We're gonna get our lives back. The nightmare is over.

speaker: Sulley
text: Hey, it's OK, Boo.
parenthetical: (Mike turns around and sees Sulley.)

speaker: Mike
text: Hey, what's the matter? C'mon, it's time to move.
parenthetical: (Sulley lifts Boo from under the desk.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Mike, what are you thinkin'? We can't trust Randall. He's after Boo.

speaker: Mike
text: Who cares? Let's go. This is a limited time offer.

speaker: Sulley
text: No, no. I don't like this.

speaker: Mike
text: Look, Sulley, you wanted her door, and there it is. Now, let's move!

speaker: Sulley
text: No, Mike.
parenthetical: (Sulley holds onto Boo tightly.)

speaker: Mike
text: You want me to prove everything's on the up and up? Fine! He wants the door; I get the door. He doesn't want it now.

speaker: Sulley
text: Mike, wait!

speaker: Mike
text: Paranoid delusional furball.

speaker: Sulley
text: Mike...!
parenthetical: (Mike enters the room and jumps on the bed. Suddenly, a large box covers Mike and snatches him away. Sulley gasps and Boo whimpers. Sulley quickly ducks under the desk. Randall peeks out of Boo's room and emerges with the box. Boo sees Randall and whimpers, Sulley gently shushes her. She nods and goes quiet. Randall loads the box into a tricked out hollow scream cart, slams the lid closed and heads for the hallway. Sulley and Boo watch Randall exit. Suddenly Boo slips and falls forward with a noise.)

speaker: Boo
text: Oof!
parenthetical: (Randall snaps to attention and disappears. Sulley peers out from under the desk. Randall is gone! But where is he? A piece of paper slips off a nearby desk and flutters to the ground. Sulley barely dares to breathe. On the other side of the desk, Randall silently materializes. Neither one sees the other, but Randall is moving closer to Sulley by the second. The lunch bell rings. Randall rushes off to his cart as monsters return from lunch. Sulley grabs Boo and sprints after Randall as he pushes his cart out into the hallway.)

speaker: Jerry
text: Hey, Sulley, where have you been all day? Sulley? Sulley!
parenthetical: (Sulley follows Randall through the halls, barely able to keep up. Sulley follows Randall around the corner to a dead end.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Huh?
parenthetical: (Disassembled scream carts and tools sit idle; Randall is nowhere to be seen. Sulley checks the carts for Mike.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Mike? Mike? You in there...? Where are ya, buddy...? Mike...
parenthetical: (Meanwhile, Boo plays with some tools hanging on a work panel. Tugging on a wrench, Boo accidentally opens a secret panel, revealing a dark corridor.)

speaker: Boo
text: Da gey makada bol!
parenthetical: [gibberish]

speaker: Sulley
text: Boo! Way to go!
parenthetical: (Boo giggles, thanking Sulley for his congratulation to her.)

speaker: Sulley
text: It's okay.
parenthetical: (The door slams shut behind him. Sulley continues. He hears the echoey voices of Randall and Fungus, which grow clearer as Sulley gets closer to them.)

speaker: Fungus
text: Um, so to confirm, then, you did find the ah...

speaker: Randall
text: Yes! I got the kid!

speaker: Fungus
text: Oh, huzzah! That's great news. N-n-not that I was concerned of course, I knew--

speaker: Randall
text: Just get over here and help me!
parenthetical: (Sulley and Boo peer through a wall of pipes. They see Randall, Fungus and the scream cart.)

speaker: Randall
text: Come on, come on, come on! While we're young here, Fungus!
parenthetical: (Randall and Fungus lift the box out of the scream cart.)

speaker: Randall
text: Kid needs to take off a few pounds.
parenthetical: (They dump the box out into a chair, revealing Mike. This is not what Randall was expecting.)

speaker: Randall
text: Wazowski! Where is it, ya little one-eyed crettin?!

speaker: Mike
text: Okay. First of all, it's cretin. If you're gonna threaten me, do it properly. Second of all, you're nuts if you think kidnapping me is gonna help you cheat your way to the top!

speaker: Randall
text: You still think this is about that stupid scare record?

speaker: Mike
text: Well... I did. Right up until... you chuckled like that... and, uh, now I'm thinkin' I should just get outta here.
parenthetical: (But before Mike could leave the handle goes in front of him to prevent him from escaping. Randall clamps Mike into the chair.)

speaker: Randall
text: I'm about to revolutionize the scaring industry. And when I do, even the "great" James P. Sullivan's gonna be working for me.

speaker: Mike
text: Well, somebody's certainly been a busy bee.

speaker: Randall
text: First I need to know where the kid is, and you're gonna tell me.

speaker: Mike
text: Hey, I don't know anything!

speaker: Randall
text: Uh-huh. Sure.
parenthetical: (Randall nods at Fungus, who presses buttons on the console.) | [unconvincingly]

speaker: Mike
text: I-I don't! I mean, I don't.... Uh-oh.
parenthetical: (A large vacuum-like machine lowers from the ceiling.)

speaker: Mike
text: What's that? Wait. Come on. Wait, wait, wait, wait. Uh-oh. Uh-oh. Oh, come on. No, no, no, no, no, no. Come on. Hey, hey, hey, hey. This thing is moving.
parenthetical: (The machine comes closer to Randall and Mike.)

speaker: Mike
text: I don't like big, moving things that are moving towards me. No! Come on! Hey! Randall!

speaker: Randall
text: Say hello to the scream extractor.
parenthetical: [patting the machine]

speaker: Mike
text: Hello.
parenthetical: (Randall smiles as he walks away from Mike.)

speaker: Mike
text: Come on. Where are you goin'? We-we'll talk. Come on, we'll have a latte. Come on. We can talk about this.
parenthetical: (Randall activates the machine. An oxygen-like mask inches towards Mike's face.)

speaker: Mike
text: What's that thing? What is that thing? Wait, wait, wait! No, no, no! Uh-oh! No! Come on, hey!
parenthetical: (Mike screams. The device is now inches away from his face. Behind the pipes, Sulley backs into the darkness with Boo, desperate to save his friend before his end.)

speaker: Mike
text: Help! Help!! Help!!! Help!!!! Oh...NO!!! WAAAH!!!
parenthetical: (The machine's suction pulls Mike's lips closer, then suddenly stops, powering down with a whir. Mike sighs, relieved. Randall groans and turns angrily on Fungus.)

speaker: Randall
text: Oh, for.... what did you do wrong this time?

speaker: Fungus
text: I don't know. I calibrated the drive shaft--

speaker: Randall
text: GO CHECK THE MACHINE!
parenthetical: [yells]

speaker: Fungus
text: There must be something wrong with the scream intake valve. That's the problem with these 3250 units...
parenthetical: (Randall sighs, frustrated. On the back of the console, he sees the cords wiggling.)

speaker: Randall
text: Huh?
parenthetical: (He slivers like a savage snake as he follows the cords along the floor around the corner...)

speaker: Randall
text: Hmm...
parenthetical: (Randall sees that the machine has been unplugged. Fungus adjusts the machine. Mike whispers to him.)

speaker: Mike
text: Psst. Fungus. Fungus! You like cars? Huh? 'Cause I got a really nice car. If you let me go, I'll give you... a ride in the car. Please, Fungus?

speaker: Fungus
text: I'm sorry, Wazowski, but Randall said I'm not allowed to fraternize with victims of his evil plot.
parenthetical: (Fungus is suddenly pulled up into the ceiling by Sulley's hands. Mike smiles in recognition. Randall plugs the machine back in. It powers up the machines. Randall enters, only to see Fungus strapped into the machine instead of Mike. The screams were now being sucked out of Fungus. Randall was really furious now.)

speaker: Randall
text: What happened?! Where's Wazowski?!
parenthetical: (Fungus struggles and grows pale as the machine does its work. Randall angrily hits a switch on the console, turning it off.) | [raging; in fury]

speaker: Randall
text: Where is he?!
parenthetical: (A nearby albino Fungus points weakly towards the exit. Growling, Randall storms off, determined to get to the bottom of this. Fungus collapses.) | [still raging]

speaker: Sulley
text: Come on.

speaker: Mike
text: This is crazy. He's gonna kill us!
parenthetical: (They nearly bump into a group of CDA agents, inspecting a trash can.)

speaker: CDA agent
text: Careful. That could be contaminated.
parenthetical: (Mike, Sulley and Boo do a quick detour, avoiding the CDA.)
gender_rationale: grouped numbered CDA agents (CDA agent #N); individual agents not distinguished, gender not stated

speaker: Mike
text: We gotta get out of here now! We can start a whole new life somewhere far away! Goodbye, Monsters, Inc.! Goodbye, Mr. Waternoose!
parenthetical: (Mike runs through a side exit door, out of the factory. With that mention of the name, Sulley stops.)

speaker: Sulley
text: No, Mike, wait.
parenthetical: (Sulley turns back the way they came.)

speaker: Mike
text: Hey, what are you doing?

speaker: Sulley
text: Follow me. I have an idea.

speaker: Mike
text: No, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no!

speaker: Waternoose
text: No, no, no, no, no!
parenthetical: [Bile is standing on one leg, sticking his tongue out at the ANIMATRONIC KID.]

speaker: Waternoose
text: What was that? You're trying to scare the kid, not lull it to sleep!

speaker: Bile
text: I was going for a snake/ninja approach with a little hissing.

speaker: Waternoose
text: How many times do I have to tell you? It's all about PRESENCE! About how you enter the room!
parenthetical: [Sulley bursts into the room holding Boo, Mike follows.]

speaker: Sulley
text: Mr. Waternoose!

speaker: Waternoose
text: James! Perfect timing.

speaker: Sulley
text: No, no, no, no. Sir, you don't understand!

speaker: Waternoose
text: Ah, now, show these monsters how it's done.

speaker: Sulley
text: I can't... Sir, sir, you have to listen to me!
parenthetical: [Waternoose grabs Boo from Sulley and hands her to Mike, much to his disgust.]

speaker: Waternoose
text: Pay attention, everyone. You're about to see the best in the business.
parenthetical: [to the recruits]

speaker: Sulley
text: Sir, I...!

speaker: Waternoose
text: Reset the simulator.

speaker: Sulley
text: But-But, sir!
parenthetical: (Waternoose positions Sulley on the stage and steps behind the control panel. The lights in the room dim, and the animatronic kid resets.)

speaker: Mother
text: Good night, sweetheart.

speaker: Little Boy
text: Good night, mom.

speaker: Father
text: Sleep tight, kiddo.
parenthetical: (Mike sets Boo down. She immediately runs towards Sulley on the stage.)

speaker: Boo
text: Kitty!

speaker: Mike
text: No, Boo, no, no!

speaker: Waternoose
text: Now, give us a big, loud roar.

speaker: Sulley
text: Mr. Waternoose, there's no time for this!

speaker: Waternoose
text: Come on, come on, what are you waiting for? Roar!

speaker: Sulley
text: But-but-but, sir!
parenthetical: (Waternoose is determined.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: ROAR!
parenthetical: (It's no use. With no other choice, he starts to...)

speaker: Sulley
text: Uhhhh!
parenthetical: (And a mighty roar it is. The animatronic screams. By now, Boo has reached the simulator room, close enough to feel the full impact of Sulley's roar. His glaring eyes and snarling teeth are genuinely horrifying. Sulley has become a gruesome, terrible monster. Terrified, Boo's face widens in fear, and she runs to hide. The recruits are impressed.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: Well done. Well done, James.
parenthetical: (Sulley sees her leave.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Boo?
parenthetical: (Waternoose and Ms. Flint escort the recruits out of the room.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: All right, gentlemen.

speaker: Ms. Flint
text: Right this way, everyone.

speaker: Waternoose
text: I hope you learned a valuable lesson in scaring today. Read chapters 2 and 3 in your manuals. I'll see you all in the morning.
parenthetical: (Sulley finds Boo, hiding in the shadows while Waternoose is talking to Flint and the 3 Pupils in distance, Worried)

speaker: Sulley
text: Boo?
parenthetical: (Sulley reaches for Boo, trying to calm and reassure her.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Boo? Boo, it's me.
parenthetical: [She backs away from Sulley and trips over a cable. Her HOOD FLOPS OPEN, revealing her SCARED FACE.]

speaker: Waternoose
text: The child!

speaker: Mike
text: Sir, she isn't toxic. I know it sounds crazy but trust me.

speaker: Sulley
text: Boo? No, no, no, no. It's okay. I was just...

speaker: Sulley
text: Oh, no, no, no, no, no, don't be scared. That wasn't real. It's just a... I was just... (Behind Boo is the bank of monitors, displaying a still image of Sulley's huge roar with his ferocious fangs glimmering like knives. This is the way hundreds of children see him and the other monsters every night in their rooms. This is what Boo saw. And for the first time in monster history, Sulley finally realized that scaring children was nothing to be proud of or be congratulated about. It's all wrong. Sulley is distraught and dumbfounded. He looks down on Boo.]

speaker: Sulley
text: Boo.
parenthetical: (She ran from Sulley towards Waternoose, hiding behind one of his crab legs.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Boo?

speaker: Mike
text: ...this scream machine and he was gonna test it out on that sweet, little girl.
parenthetical: (Mike is still explaining the situation to Waternoose.)

speaker: Mike
text: Now that we have her, he is trying to kill us! This whole thing is Randall's fault!

speaker: Waternoose
text: Randall?

speaker: Mike
text: Yes. And we can take you to his secret lab, which is right here in this factory.
parenthetical: (Waternoose is stunned. He lets this information sink in.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: How could this happen? Oh, how could this happen? Does anyone else know about this?

speaker: Mike
text: No, sir.

speaker: Waternoose
text: Good. This company can't afford any more bad publicity. Now, before we do anything else, let's...take care of the child.
parenthetical: (Sulley watches her with remorse, closing his eyes and guiltily hanging his head. Waternoose flips the switch, and the simulator door ejects, just like the Scare Floor. It travels away on an overhead track.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: Oh, I never thought things would come to this, not in my factory. I'm sorry you boys got mixed up in this. Especially you, James. But now we can set everything straight again, for the good of the company.

speaker: Mike
text: Uh, sir, that's not her door.

speaker: Waternoose
text: I know, I know.
parenthetical: (Randall materializes in front of the door and opens it with an evil smile. Snow swirls through from the other side.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: It's yours.
parenthetical: (Sulley and Mike both gasp in horror. Their old friend is a traitor! Waternoose shoves the two monsters through the door.) | [evilly]

speaker: Sulley and Mike
text: AAAAAHHH!!
parenthetical: (A blizzard rages. Sulley and Mike land face down in the snow. A grumpy Waternoose stands in the doorway, still holding Boo.)

speaker: Sulley
text: NO!!!
parenthetical: (Sulley desperately makes a run for the door, but Waternoose shuts the door. Sulley pulls the door open and sees only more snow. The portal to the monster world is closed!)

speaker: Sulley
text: BOOOOOOOOOOOOO!!!!!!
parenthetical: (Mike and Sulley are in the middle of a snowy mountain range. Panicked, Sulley opens and slams the door, but nothing changes.)

speaker: Sulley
text: No! No! NO, NO, NO, NO, NO!!!
parenthetical: (Sulley desperately runs his hands along the sides of the door, looking for anything that might activate it. Behind him, Mike watches angrily.)

speaker: Mike
text: It's too late! We're BANISHED, genius!! We're in the HUMAN WORLD!!! Oh, what a great idea; goin' to your old pal Waternoose! Too bad he was in on the whole thing! All you hadda do, was listen to me. JUST ONCE!!! But you DIDN'T, did you?!
parenthetical: (Sulley's not listening. All he can think about is Boo.)

speaker: Mike
text: YOU'RE STILL NOT LISTENING!!!!! AAAAAAAAGGGGHHH!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
parenthetical: (Mike violently leaps onto Sulley's back, sending both of them tumbling down the side of the snowy mountain.) | [voice rising higher with anger]

speaker: Sulley and Mike
text: AAHHH!!!!!
parenthetical: (Mike continues to pound at Sulley as they flip over an embankment, landing with a thud several yards below.)

speaker: Mike
text: Take that!
parenthetical: (Sulley roars as he fights back, pinning Mike down. The two growl and raise fists, about to punch each other. Suddenly, a giant shadow covers them. They look up to see a yeti! Known as an Abominable Snowman.)

speaker: Yeti
text: Welcome to the Himalayas!
parenthetical: (Mike and Sulley trade a side glance. Mike huddles around the lantern for warmth because he's cold from being out in the snow for so long. He's wearing mittens on his horns, his hands, and his feet.)

speaker: Yeti
text: Abominable! Can you believe that? Do I look abominable to you? Why can't they call me the Adorable Snowman, or... or the Agreeable Snowman, for cryin' out loud? I'm a nice guy. Snow cone?
parenthetical: (The Yeti holds out a tray of yellow snow cones. Any astute person will realize that the snow cones are NOT lemon flavored.)

speaker: Mike
text: Yuck!

speaker: Yeti
text: Oh, no, no, no. Don't worry. It's lemon. How 'bout you, big fella? Snow cone?
parenthetical: (A depressed Sulley sits at the mouth of the cave, staring at his hands.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Did you see the way she... looked at me?
parenthetical: (In anguish, he buries his face in his hands.)

speaker: Yeti
text: Ah, poor guy. I understand. It ain't easy bein' banished. Take my buddy, Bigfoot. When he was banished, he fashioned an enormous diaper out of poison ivy. Wore it on his head like a tiara. Called himself "King Itchy".
parenthetical: (Mike looks up at the Yeti as though he's insane.)

speaker: Yeti
text: It won't be so hard for you guys, though, you know, I mean, how lucky can you get? Banished with your best friend.

speaker: Mike
text: He is not my friend.

speaker: Yeti
text: Oh, I just assumed you were buddies, you know, when I saw you out there in the snow huggin' and all that...
parenthetical: (Mike stares angrily at Sulley.)

speaker: Mike
text: Look at that big jerk. Ruined my life, and for what? A STUPID KID!!! Because of YOU, I am now STUCK in this frozen wasteland!

speaker: Yeti
text: Wasteland? I think you mean "Wonderland"! I mean, how 'bout all this fabulous snow, huh? Oh, and wait 'til you see the local village - cutest thing in the world. I haven't even mentioned all the free yak's milk...
parenthetical: (For the first time, Sulley turns, his eyes alive.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Wait, what did you say?

speaker: Yeti
text: Uh, yak's milk. Milkin' a yak ain't exactly a picnic, but you know, once you pick the hairs out, it's very nutritious.
parenthetical: (Sulley jumps up, runs across the cave, and faces the Yeti.)

speaker: Sulley
text: No, no! Something about a village! Where?! Are there kids in it?!

speaker: Yeti
text: Kids? Sure. Tough kids, sissy kids, kids who climb on rocks...
parenthetical: (This diversion is more than Sulley can handle.)

speaker: Sulley
text: WHERE IS IT?!

speaker: Yeti
text: Hey, hey. It's at the bottom of the mountain. Around a three-day hike.

speaker: Sulley
text: Ugh, three days?! We need to get there NOW!!!
parenthetical: (Sulley punches the wall in frustration, sending an icicle across the floor. It stops in front of a pile of hiker paraphernalia piled in the corner of the cave. Sulley's eyes light up. He has an idea.)

speaker: Yeti
text: You wanna go to the village? OK, rule number one out here: Always...no. Never go out in a blizzard.

speaker: Sulley
text: We need to get to Boo.
parenthetical: (SMACK! A snowball hits Sulley in the back. The Yeti points to Mike nervously - he did it.)

speaker: Mike
text: BOO?! WHAT ABOUT US?! Ever since that kid came in, you've ignored everything I've said, and NOW look where we are! Oh, we were about to break the record, Sulley! We would've had it made!

speaker: Sulley
text: None of that matters now.
parenthetical: (Mike stops, stunned by what he has just heard.)

speaker: Mike
text: None of it matters? Wa-wa-wait a second. "None of it matters"?
parenthetical: (Sulley doesn't answer.)

speaker: Mike
text: Okay, that's - no. Good. Great. So now the truth comes out, doesn't it?
parenthetical: (There is an uncomfortable pause.)

speaker: Yeti
text: Oh, ho, would you look at that? We're out of snow cones. Let me... just go outside and make some more.
parenthetical: (The Yeti leaves.)

speaker: Mike
text: Sulley, what about everything we ever worked for? Does that matter? Huh? What about Celia? I am never... never gonna see her again. Doesn't that matter?
parenthetical: (Sulley has finished building a makeshift toboggan. In silence, he slowly pushes it past Mike.)

speaker: Mike
text: And what about me? I'm your pal, I'm-I'm-I'm your best friend. Don't I matter?

speaker: Sulley
text: I'm sorry, Mike. I'm sorry we're stuck out here. I didn't mean for this to happen. But Boo's in trouble. I think there might be a way to save her, if we can just get down to that--

speaker: Mike
text: "We"? Whoa, whoa, whoa. "We"? No. There's no "we" this time, pal. If-if-if-if you wanna go out there and freeze to death, you be my guest. Because you're on your own.
parenthetical: (Mike turns his back on Sulley, folding his arms. There is nothing Sulley can say. He turns and pushes the toboggan out of the cave. The cave grows dark. Mike stares in disbelief. Sulley is gone. And Mike regrets it.) | (Sulley races down the mountainside on his makeshift sled. The lantern dangles from a ski pole on the sled, lighting the way as Sulley navigates the treacherous terrain. He zips by the Yeti, folding a fresh tray of snow.)

speaker: Yeti
text: Hey, I got more snow cones!
parenthetical: (The sled is narrowly missing boulders, and picking up speed until...)

speaker: Sulley
text: Ah!!
parenthetical: (Sulley's sled smashes into a boulder, and bursts apart. He tumbles down the mountainside, finally sliding to a stop, face down in the snow. Sulley lies motionless in the snow, the wind howling through his fur. A child screams in the distance. Or was that the wind? Sulley slowly lifted his head. Another scream, this one louder than the first. Sulley's eyes widen. Through the snow, Sulley can barely make out a small Tibetan village.)

speaker: Charlie
text: Oh, come on, now, George. I know you can do this. I picked out an easy door for you, in Nepal. Nice, quiet Nepal.

speaker: George
text: You know, you're right. Here, take this.

speaker: Charlie
text: Go get 'em, Georgie.
parenthetical: (As George gets to the door, Sulley bursts through, knocking George over.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Gangway! Look out! Comin' through!
parenthetical: (George lies flattened on the ground.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Sorry, George.

speaker: Charlie
text: What the--? Hey! You can't just-
parenthetical: (As George staggers to his feet, Charlie spots a sock stuck to his chest. Charlie's eyes go wide.)

speaker: Charlie
parenthetical: (Before Charlie can finish, George grabs him by the throat, stuffs the sock in his mouth, and tosses him in the door. George relaxes, whistling happily as he walks away. Sulley runs past various monsters in the hallway.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Outta the way!

speaker: Miscellaneous Monster
text: Hey! Whoa! Don't!
parenthetical: (Randall straps Boo into the scream extractor chair. She whimpers as Waternoose looks on.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: Finally! I never should have trusted you with this. Because of you, I had to banish my top scarer!

speaker: Randall
text: Eh, with this machine, we won't need scarers. Besides, Sullivan got what he deserved.

speaker: Waternoose
text: Sullivan was TWICE the scarer you'll ever be!
parenthetical: (Randall glares at Waternoose with a growl of fury. Sulley rips off the door to the secret passageway and runs through. Fungus revs up the machine. Waternoose and Randall watch with anticipation as the machine nears Boo's face. In fear, she calls out for Sulley.)

speaker: Boo
text: Kitty!
parenthetical: (While Boo was screaming in horror, Sulley rushes in, roaring angrily and heroically. He grabs the Scream Extractor and forces it away from Boo.)

speaker: Boo
text: Kitty!
parenthetical: (The villainous trio look as if they've seen a ghost.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: Sullivan?
parenthetical: (With a massive effort, Sulley wrenches the machine from its moorings and with another roar, he throws it towards Randall, Waternoose and Fungus. It pins them to the wall, except for Randall who manages to dodge it.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: Wh-whoa! UGH!!!
parenthetical: (Sulley frees Boo from the chair.)

speaker: Boo
text: Kitty!

speaker: Sulley
text: Sorry, Boo.
parenthetical: (Waternoose angrily turns to Randall, who has managed to escape.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: STOP HIM!
parenthetical: (Randall growls with rage at Sulley for wrecking his machine and camouflages, disappearing. Sulley picks Boo up out of the chair.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Let's get you home.
parenthetical: (Sulley picks up Boo's card key and runs towards the exit. Suddenly, Sulley is knocked back into the room, as if punched by nothing.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: Finish him off!
parenthetical: (Sulley keeps getting punched. He can't see where it's coming from. Behind him, a scream can rises into the air and hits him on the head. Sulley falls to the ground, still holding Boo. Randall materializes, hanging from the ceiling.)

speaker: Randall
text: You don't know how long I've wanted to do that, Sullivan!
parenthetical: (Sulley swings at Randall to fight back and misses. Using his tail as a whip and with a wicked chuckle, Randall sends Sulley flying out into the hallway. Sulley lands with a thud. Boo spills out of his arms. She quickly hides in a crevice in the hallway. Sulley is hit in the face with a snowball. He turns to see...)

speaker: Sulley
text: Ooh! Mike?

speaker: Mike
text: Look, it's not that I don't care about the kid.

speaker: Sulley
text: Mike, you don't understand.
parenthetical: (Mid-sentence, Sulley gets attacked by an invisible Randall once again. Mike continues his apology, oblivious.)

speaker: Mike
text: Yes, I do. I was just mad, that's all. I needed some time to think, but you shouldn't have left me out there.
parenthetical: (Sulley is slammed against the wall.)

speaker: Sulley
text: I'm being attacked!

speaker: Mike
text: No, I'm not attacking you. I'm tryna be honest, just hear me out. Look, you and I are a team. Nothin' is more important than our friendship.
parenthetical: (Boo tugs on Mike's arm and points at the invisible Randall, trying to tell him that Sulley is being attacked by him but Mike doesn't understand her.)

speaker: Mike
text: I-I know, kid. He's too sensitive.
parenthetical: (Invisible Randall chokes Sulley, who wheezes and gasps for air.)

speaker: Mike
text: Come on, pal, if you start cryin', I'm gonna cry, and I'll never get through this.
parenthetical: (Sulley grabs futilely at his neck and struggles as hard as he could.)

speaker: Mike
text: I'm sorry I wasn't there for you, but I am now.
parenthetical: (Sulley is about to black out.)

speaker: Mike
text: Hey, Sulley, I am baring my soul here. The least you can do is pay attention!
parenthetical: (Mike hurls his snowball towards Sulley, but instead, it smashes Randall's invisible face, exposing him to view. Seeing his chance, Sulley quickly makes a mighty fist and as fast as lighting, he punches Randall extremely hard with tremendous force, knocking him out cold.)

speaker: Randall
text: ORRGH!!!
parenthetical: (Out of dazed and seeing stars, Randall unconsciously collapses to the ground, becoming visible again and releasing Sulley in progress. Sulley puts his other hand to his neck and gasps for breath and sighs in relief, finally out of Randall's grip and also glad he survived. Now that was close.)

speaker: Mike
text: Hey, look at that, it's Randall. It's... Oh.
parenthetical: (Still breathing, Sulley picks up Boo and Mike and runs out.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Come on!
parenthetical: (Randall is beginning to come, too, though still dazed by the blow. He shakes his head to come to his senses. Waternoose yells at him from behind the machine.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: GET UP! There can't be any witnesses!
parenthetical: (Randall lifts himself off the ground)

speaker: Randall
text: There won't be.

speaker: Sulley
text: I'm glad you came back, Mike.

speaker: Mike
text: Somebody's gotta take care of ya, ya big hairball.
parenthetical: (A horrible scream gets Mike's attention. It's Celia! Snakes flowing wildly, she leaps through the air and tackles him to the ground.)

speaker: Mike
text: Schmoopsie-Poo, I really can't talk.

speaker: Sulley
text: Come on!
parenthetical: (Sulley drags Mike away. Celia isn't about to be left behind again. She grabs Mike's leg, and so is dragged after him.)

speaker: Celia
text: Michael, if you don't tell me what's going on right now, we are through! Do you hear me? Through!

speaker: Mike
text: Okay, here's the truth! You know that kid that they're lookin' for? Sulley let her in! We tried to send her back, but Waternoose had this secret plot, and now Randall's right behind us, and he's trying to kill us!

speaker: Celia
text: YOU EXPECT ME TO BELIEVE THAT PACK OF LIES, MIKE WAZOWSKI?!
parenthetical: (Boo pops up from behind Sulley's shoulder.)

speaker: Boo
text: Mike Wazowski!
parenthetical: (Celia shrieks. In terror, she lets go of Mike as he and Sulley round the corner towards the Scare Floor.)

speaker: Mike
text: I love you, Schmoopsie-Poo!
parenthetical: (Randall and Fungus come running down the hall, crashing after Sulley and Mike. They trip over a slow monster pushing a scream cart, knocking over the cans as Randall leaps over the cart like a savage leopard.)

speaker: Randall
text: Will you move it?! Look out, you... idiots!!!
parenthetical: (Celia's eye widens, then narrows in determination.) | (Sulley and Mike run onto the crowded Scare Floor and head for an empty station.)

speaker: Mike
text: Look out. Comin' through here! Comin' through! Make way. Move it!

speaker: Sulley
text: Sorry.

speaker: Mike
text: Hurry up, hurry up!
parenthetical: (They swipe Boo's card key and wait anxiously for her door to arrive. Randall and Fungus run out onto the Scare Floor.)

speaker: Randall
text: There they are!
parenthetical: (Sulley and Mike turn at the sound of Randall's voice.)

speaker: Celia
text: Attention, employees: Randall Boggs has just broken the all-time scare record!
parenthetical: (Randall and Fungus are mobbed by a collection of excited employees, who trap the duo.)

speaker: Randall
text: Huh? No, I didn't. Get out of my way!

speaker: Employee Walla
text: Way to go, Randall! Nice job!
parenthetical: (Celia hangs up the intercom phone with a smile. Success!)

speaker: Celia
text: Go get 'em, Googly Bear.
parenthetical: (Sulley and Mike watch as Fungus and Randall struggle to break free of the crowd. Boo squeals happily as her door enters the Scare Floor on the conveyor belt above.)

speaker: Mike
text: There it is!
parenthetical: (Meanwhile Fungus is being tossed happily into the air by the celebratory group.)

speaker: Fungus
text: Whoa! Ha-ha!

speaker: Randall
text: Get off my tail! Let me through!
parenthetical: (Randall is finally able to push his way through the mob. He heads towards Sulley. Boo's door will not reach their station before Randall. Sulley punches a red panic button on the keypad. An alarm sounds and Boo's door, poised high above the station, heads back into the door vault. Sulley runs toward the next station over. All the doors are being returned to the vault.)

speaker: Mike
text: Sulley, what are you doing?!

speaker: Sulley
text: Grab on, Mike!
parenthetical: (With Boo in one hand, Sulley grabs the door as it sails out of its station. Mike manages to grab onto Sulley's tail.)

speaker: Mike
text: Are you out of your... ...MIIIIIIIIND?!
parenthetical: (Randall watches the trio cling to the door hanging overhead. He climbs aboard a door being ejected and sails up onto the track several doors behind Sulley, Mike and Boo. The two dozen doors glide towards the vault. Sulley, Mike and Boo follow Boo's door down a long corridor.)

speaker: Mike
text: Sulley, what are we doing?!

speaker: Sulley
text: We have to get Boo's door and find a station.

speaker: Mike
text: What a plan. Simple, yet insane!
parenthetical: (Randall hangs from a door. He leaps forward one door, closing the gap. The corridor opens up into a massive room. Millions of doors, as far as the eye can see, travel on overhead tracks.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Whoa.

speaker: Mike
text: Oh, boy.
parenthetical: (Their door suddenly pitches forward.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Hold on!
parenthetical: (They speed through the vault as if on a roller coaster ride.)

speaker: Sulley and Mike
text: Aaaaaahhhh!!

speaker: Mike
text: Whoaaaaa! Wowww!

speaker: Sulley
text: Don't look down!
parenthetical: (The door pitches and twists dangerously as it is switched from track to track, Boo enjoys the ride. The door turns, suddenly going into a 45 degree drop.)

speaker: Sulley and Mike
text: AAAAAAAAAAAAHHHHHHHHHHHH!!!!!!
parenthetical: (Boo screams happily, causing the red light to generate power on the door they're riding, which surges, and finally overpowers as Boo's voice increases even more. In a moment, it then fades off, losing power along with Boo being quiet. The track rounds a post.)

speaker: Mike
text: I'm gonna be sick, I'm gonna be sick! Whoaaaaa! Oh, no!
parenthetical: (Ahead, a switch reroutes the doors onto multiple tracks, Boo's door on one, theirs on another.)

speaker: Sulley
text: No!
parenthetical: (Randall's door switches onto yet another track. Sulley visually follows Boo's door. It stops, stacked against the wall of doors.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Boo's door!

speaker: Mike
text: There it is! How are we supposed to get to it now?!
parenthetical: (Their door travels into a corridor of doors, surrounded on all slides, enveloping them in darkness. It slows to a stop.)

speaker: Mike
text: Oh, it's a dead end, Sulley!
parenthetical: (From the other end of the tunnel of doors, Randall jumps onto a door and rides it towards them.)

speaker: Mike
text: There he is!
parenthetical: (Sulley gets an idea.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Make her laugh!

speaker: Mike
text: What? Sulley!

speaker: Sulley
text: JUST DO IT!

speaker: Mike
text: Oh...
parenthetical: (Mike pulls his eyelid out and snaps it back on his eye.)

speaker: Mike
text: OW!
parenthetical: (As Boo squeals with laughter, the light above their door illuminates. The doors surrounding them activate too. In fact, every door in the vault powers up, their lights glowing bright red. By now Randall is almost upon them.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Get it open!

speaker: Mike
text: Here he comes!
parenthetical: (The trio jump inside and shut their door just as Randall leaps towards them.)

speaker: Randall
text: Gimme that kid!!! Oof!
parenthetical: (It was a beautiful tranquil day in the tropics. Sulley, Mike and Boo run out of a Hawaiian beach house, onto the sand.)

speaker: Mike
text: Why couldn't we get banished here?

speaker: Sulley
text: Come on, we gotta find another door!
parenthetical: (They head onto a nearby beach house. Sulley, Mike and Boo burst out of a door, hanging thousands of feet in the air.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Whoa!
parenthetical: (Doors are stacked in rows above and below them, like a wall.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Look! Boo's door!
parenthetical: (He climbs from one door to the next, trying to reach Boo's door. Mike sees Randall approaching from the Hawaiian door. He shuts the door and shimmies up toward Sulley)

speaker: Mike
text: There he is! Hurry! Hurry up!

speaker: Sulley
text: Gimme your hand!
parenthetical: (Mike's hand slips from Sulley's grasp, but he grabs Mike's foot. Mike hangs precariously over the vault, thousands of feet in the air.)

speaker: Mike
text: Aaaaaaah! AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAHH!! Oh-oh, whoa-a! AAAAAAAAHHH!!!!!!!
parenthetical: (Sulley pulls Mike up into the door with him just as Randall emerges from Hawaii. Mike and Sulley come to a Japanese paper door. Their silhouettes are visible as they pause in front of it.)

speaker: Mike
text: Come on, it slides, it slides!

speaker: Sulley
text: Oh right, right-right!
parenthetical: (Sulley slides the door open, and they run through the room past a view of Mt. Fuji. They emerge again, this time through a door moving along the track.)

speaker: Mike
text: Jump! I'm right behind ya!
parenthetical: (Sulley jumps onto another track of doors below. Mike follows. They land on a platform.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Come on!
parenthetical: (Mike looks back and sees Randall approaching.)

speaker: Mike
text: Hurry up! Keep moving!
parenthetical: (The only available door is on the ground lying flat, undergoing some sort of maintenance. Sulley opens the door and motions for Mike to jump in.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Get inside!
parenthetical: (Because the door was lying sideways, Mike's fall changes trajectory as he enters a French kid's room. He falls sideways onto the floor.)

speaker: Mike
text: Oof! That was weird. UGH!
parenthetical: (Sulley falls through, landing on Mike.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Mike? Ooh. Sorry, buddy.

speaker: Mike
text: Ah, okay.
parenthetical: (Mike and Sulley run through the room and exit. Randall runs across the platform and jumps through the door on the ground. Randall lands in the same French kid's room, then continues after Mike and Sulley. Randall emerges. The door slams in his face. Mike appears behind it.)

speaker: Mike
text: I hope that hurt, lizard boy!
parenthetical: (Mike jumps over to another door with Sulley and Boo. The three ride off across the vault.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Great job, buddy! We lost him!
parenthetical: (Suddenly Boo slips through Sulley's arms and disappears, Boo screams.)

speaker: Sulley
text: BOO!
parenthetical: (Randall appears atop the door, holding Boo in his many arms.)

speaker: Randall
text: Aha!

speaker: Sulley
text: NO!
parenthetical: (Randall pulls out a pin from their door, sending it plummeting downward. Screaming, Mike and Sulley stare up at Randall in disbelief as they fall.)

speaker: Sulley and Mike
text: AAAAAAAH!!
parenthetical: (Randall smiles down at them)

speaker: Randall
text: Nice workin' with ya!
parenthetical: (Sulley and Mike struggle to hang onto the door as it plunges downward.)

speaker: Sulley
text: GET IT OPEN!

speaker: Mike
text: I'M TRYING!!!

speaker: Sulley
text: OPEN THE DOOR!
parenthetical: (Mike is able to open the door and clamor in to the bedroom inside.)

speaker: Mike
text: COME ON, GET IN HERE!
parenthetical: (Mike pulls Sulley through and shuts the door just seconds before it smashes into tiny pieces on the floor, Randall jumps from door to door, holding Boo. A pile of partially assembled doors starts to move, as if someone were stacked within it. The top door pops open and Sulley jumps out, followed by Mike. They run to the edge of the platform.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Boo!
parenthetical: (Sulley spots a little purple figure riding a door off in the distance.)

speaker: Sulley
text: There they are!
parenthetical: (He runs to the end of the platform which sticks out precariously over a sea of moving doors like the prow of a ship. Sulley climbs up and balances on its metal railing.)

speaker: Mike
text: Sulley, what are you doing? Sulley!
parenthetical: (Timing is just right, Sulley leaps out onto a passing door. Mike follows, jumping onto the door behind him. Their doors join up with the "freeway" of doors above, speeding ahead.)

speaker: Mike
text: Looks like we caught the express, pal!
parenthetical: (Sulley desperately scans the doors ahead.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Do you see 'em?

speaker: Mike
text: Straight ahead!
parenthetical: (Sure enough, Randall is heading towards the exit with Boo in tow. Sulley jumps onto a faster moving door on an adjacent track. He jumps a second time but loses his grip, almost falling. The door tilts towards another track, banging into oncoming doors as they zip by.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Whoa! Aaah!
parenthetical: (Randall hears the commotion and turns around. He spots Sulley, regaining his balance and closing in. Randall opens the door he's riding on and slithers into the room. Sulley uses his door like a pendulum to swing forward into the room after him. Sulley bursts through the door and spots Boo standing alone in the middle of the moonlit room.)

speaker: Boo
text: Kitty!

speaker: Sulley
text: Boo! Aah! Aaah!
parenthetical: (Randall stands menacingly over Sulley, who holds onto the bottom of the door for dear life.)

speaker: Randall
text: Look at everybody's favorite scarer now, you stupid, pathetic waste! You've been Number One for too long, Sullivan! Now your time is up! And don't worry. I'll take good care of the KID.

speaker: Sulley
text: No!
parenthetical: (Suddenly, something yanks Randall's head back.)

speaker: Randall
text: Yaaaaah! Ow! Yowch! Aah! Ow!
parenthetical: (Sulley brutally grabs Randall by the neck, strangling him in return and wrestling him in his arms like an alligator. Choking, Randall writhes and wiggles, but Sulley is able to subdue him with all his strength. Boo roars at Randall.)

speaker: Sulley
text: She's not scared of you anymore. Looks like you're out of the job.
parenthetical: (Randall gulps, knowing that he's done for. Mike places a door near the edge of the platform, opens it, and crouches down in front like a baseball catcher.)

speaker: Mike
text: All right, come on, over the plate, let's see the old stuff here, pal. Come on, now, chuck him, chuck him, baby, hum, baby. Hum, baby. Here's the pitch...
parenthetical: (Sulley prepares to throw Randall through the door.)

speaker: Randall
text: NO, NO, WAIT! PLEASE! DON'T! DON'T! DON'T! NOOOOOOO!

speaker: Mike
text: And he is... outta here!
parenthetical: (A dilapidated trailer sits swamp-side. A Pizza Planet Truck is visible. We see silhouettes of a boy, his mother, and Randall.)

speaker: Redneck boy
text: Mama, another gator got in the house.

speaker: Redneck mama
text: Another gator?! Gimme that shovel! Come here!
parenthetical: (Mama beats Randall on the head with the shovel.)

speaker: Redneck boy
text: Get him, mama! Get that gator!

speaker: Randall
text: OOH! OW! AAH!!
parenthetical: (Sulley smashes the light on top of the door.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Care to do the honors, Mikey?

speaker: Mike
text: With pleasure.
parenthetical: (Mike pushes the dilapidated door over the side of the platform. It falls and smashes at the bottom of the vault. Boo waves goodbye, taunting.)

speaker: Sulley
text: That's right, Boo. You did it! You beat him.
parenthetical: (Boo gives Randall a big wet raspberry.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Come on!
parenthetical: (Sulley, Mike and Boo climb onto Boo's door.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Okay, Boo, it's time to go home. Take care of yourself and be a good girl, okay?
parenthetical: (Mike opens the door but sees only other doors stacked behind it.)

speaker: Mike
text: Oh, no!

speaker: Sulley
text: The power's out. Make her laugh again.

speaker: Mike
text: All right, I got a move here. It'll bring down the house. Up!
parenthetical: (Mike jumps gracefully into the air and does a flip, landing crotch first on top of the metal door track.)

speaker: Mike
text: Heh?
parenthetical: (Sulley notices that Boo's hood is down.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Sorry, she didn't see that.

speaker: Mike
text: What?! What'd you do, forget to check if her stupid hood was up, ya big dope?!
parenthetical: (Boo frowns. She doesn't like Mike to yell at Sulley.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Uncle Mike, try not to yell in front of her. You know we still need her to laugh.

speaker: Mike
text: Right. Hey, Boo, just kiddin'. Look!
parenthetical: (Mike closes the door on his face, squishing it and making a goofy face.)

speaker: Mike
text: Hello, Boo!
parenthetical: (Nothing from Boo, who is still not in the mood to laugh now.)

speaker: Mike
text: Funny, right? See, with the... These are the jokes, kid.
parenthetical: (Boo's door jerks violently and heads off towards the exit. Mike, Sulley and Boo hang onto the door as it moves.)

speaker: Mike
text: Whoa! What's happening?!

speaker: Sulley
text: Hold on!

speaker: Waternoose
text: When the door lands in this station, cut the power. You'll have the child, and the criminals responsible for this whole mess.
parenthetical: (Boo's door sails out of the vault corridor and into the Scare Floor. Sulley, Mike and Boo peek out from behind the door and see Waternoose and the CDA agents below.)

speaker: Mike
text: Great. A welcoming committee! What are we gonna do?
parenthetical: (Sulley has an idea. The door lands in the station. The power shuts off.)

speaker: CDA agent
text: This is the CDA. Come out slowly with the child in plain sight.
parenthetical: (Mike emerges from behind the door, carrying Boo's monster costume.)
gender_rationale: grouped numbered CDA agents (CDA agent #N); individual agents not distinguished, gender not stated

speaker: Mike
text: Okay, okay! You got us. Here we are. Here's the kid. I'm cooperatin'. But before you take us away, I have one thing to say:
parenthetical: (Mike sticks his tongue out. There's a sock on it!)

speaker: Mike
text: Catch!
parenthetical: (It lands on an agent in the middle of the pack! The frenzied CDA agents pounce on the agent with the sock.)

speaker: CDA agents
text: 23-19! We have a toxic projectile! Isolate the contaminate!
parenthetical: (Mike takes off, carrying Boo's monster disguise. The CDA agents seen this way and chase to get to help Mike.)

speaker: CDA agent #82472
text: Halt! After the suspect!
gender_rationale: grouped numbered CDA agents (CDA agent #N); individual agents not distinguished, gender not stated

speaker: CDA agent #2
text: Cover the area! Bring in reinforcements!
parenthetical: (Waternoose takes up the rear.)
gender_rationale: grouped numbered CDA agents (CDA agent #N); individual agents not distinguished, gender not stated

speaker: Waternoose
text: Stop him!
parenthetical: (As the commotion clears, Sulley peeks out from behind Boo's door. Boo is with him, now out of her disguise and wearing only one sock. Sulley pops her door out of its station and heads for the exit.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Come on.
parenthetical: (Waternoose chases after the CDA agents)

speaker: Waternoose
text: Don't let THEM GET AWAY! WHAT...?!
parenthetical: (CLANG! Waternoose spins around to see Sulley and Boo, who has knocked over a stack of cans during his escape. Sulley scoops up Boo and her door and heads for the Simulation Room.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: NO, WAIT, WAIT! COME BACK! He has the child!
parenthetical: (The agents have no choice with him and already disappeared get help Mike. Suddenly, With a frustrated growl, Waternoose lunges after Sulley like a wild animal. Sulley races down the hall, with Boo in one arm, and her door in the other. Waternoose clambers after them.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: SULLIVAN! SULLIVAN! GIVE ME THE CHILD!

speaker: Boo
text: I am not going!

speaker: Waternoose
text: GIVE HER TO ME!
parenthetical: (Sulley bursts through the door of the Simulator Room. Sulley breaks a pipe off the wall and slides it through the door handles just as Waternoose slams against the door. Sulley bends the pipe around the handles to secure it. He frantically removes the door that's currently in the station.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: Open this door! OPEN THIS DOOR!
parenthetical: (Sulley replaces the simulator door with Boo's door. Waternoose kept banging the doors, growling savagely and scratching at the side of the doors, showing that he's the true monster as he is now.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: HEY! SULLIVAN!
parenthetical: (Boo screams and braces herself in fear.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: Don't do it!
parenthetical: (Sulley pushes a button on the door station keypad, picks Boo up and runs inside.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Come on!

speaker: Waternoose
text: DON'T GO IN THAT ROOM!
parenthetical: (Sulley angrily glares at him and shuts the closet door behind him. Waternoose loses it and beats against the Simulation Room doors)

speaker: Waternoose
text: OHHH, NOOO!!!
parenthetical: (Sulley tucks Boo into bed)

speaker: Sulley
text: I think we stopped him, Boo. You're safe now. You be a good girl, okay?
parenthetical: (Sulley turns to see Waternoose like this, standing in the room, closing the door behind him.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: This has gone far enough, James.
parenthetical: (Sulley stands up to him, defensively protecting Boo.)

speaker: Sulley
text: She's home now! Just leave her alone!

speaker: Waternoose
text: I can't do that! She's seen too much. You both have.

speaker: Sulley
text: It doesn't have to be this way.

speaker: Waternoose
text: I have no choice! Times have changed! Scaring isn't enough anymore!
parenthetical: (Waternoose stands taller with his legs intimidatingly.)

speaker: Sulley
text: But kidnapping children?!

speaker: Waternoose
text: I'll kidnap a THOUSAND children before I let this company die, and I'll SILENCE ANYONE WHO GETS IN MY WAY!
parenthetical: (Waternoose shoves Sulley aside, sending him to the floor.)

speaker: Sulley
text: NO!
parenthetical: (Waternoose steals the mechanical simulation child from the bed.)

speaker: Animatronic Boy
text: Good night, mom.

speaker: Mother
text: Good night, sweetheart.

speaker: Animatronic Boy
text: Good night, mom. Good night, mom.

speaker: Waternoose
text: What, wh-what is this? What? Who? Huh?
parenthetical: (Waternoose exclaims in suprise, uncomprehending. The child isn't Boo at all, but the Animatronic Kid from the simulator. The wall of the bedroom begins to rise.)

speaker: Computerized Voice
text: Simulation terminated. Simulation terminated.
parenthetical: (Behind the fourth wall of the simulator, the simulation room is filled with yellow-suited CDA agents. Mike sits in the evaluator's chair.)

speaker: Mike
text: Well, I don't know about the rest of you guys, but I spotted several big mistakes.

speaker: Waternoose
text: B-but... H-How'd--?

speaker: Mike
text: You know what? Let's watch my favorite part again, shall we?

speaker: Waternoose
text: I'll kidnap a THOUSAND children before I let this company die! I'll kidnap a THOUSAND children before I let this company die! I'll kidnap a THOUSAND children before I let this company die!
parenthetical: (Waternoose sees himself on the monitor, watching in defeated silent. That means he's been tricked by the heroic duo to expose his true nature and identities. Sulley smiles softly, glad he's won.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: What...?! Wha...?!
parenthetical: (As the tape repeats itself, the CDA agents discuss the situation. Boo peeks out from under the bed. Sulley motions for her to stay quiet. She nods and crawls back. CDA agents walk onto the stage and take Waternoose by the arms.)

speaker: CDA agent
text: I'll get him. Alright, come with us, sir.
gender_rationale: grouped numbered CDA agents (CDA agent #N); individual agents not distinguished, gender not stated

speaker: Waternoose
text: Wh-what are you doing?! Take your hands off me! You can't arrest me!
parenthetical: (The CDA agents lead Waternoose out of the simulator room and he angrily blames Sulley whilst being dragged away. Sulley softly and calmly glares back at him, ignoring his excuses.)

speaker: Waternoose
text: I hope you're happy, Sullivan! You destroyed this company! Monsters Incorporated is dead! Where will everyone get their scream now?! The energy crisis will only get worse, because of you!
parenthetical: (Sulley doesn't care. He looks down at the floor, feeling ashamed of himself. Mike feels sorry for about all that.)

speaker: Lead CDA Agent
text: Stay where you are. Number One wants to talk to you. Attention!

speaker: Roz
text: Hello, boys.
parenthetical: [Roz enters the room]

speaker: Sulley and Mike
text: Roz?

speaker: Roz
text: Two and a half years of undercover work were almost wasted when you intercepted that child, Mr. Sullivan. Of course, without your help, I never would have known that this went all the way up to Waternoose.
parenthetical: (Boo scampers up to Sulley and Mike. Nearby CDA agents nervously step away.)

speaker: Roz
text: Now, about the girl...
parenthetical: (Sulley scoops Boo up in his arms.)

speaker: Sulley
text: I just wanna send her home.

speaker: Roz
text: Very good. Bring me a door shredder.

speaker: Sulley
text: What, you mean... You mean, I can't see her again?

speaker: Roz
text: That's the way it has to be. I'll give you five minutes.
parenthetical: (Sulley looks at Boo and sighs. Boo seems to understand.)

speaker: Mike
text: Well, so long, kid.

speaker: Boo
text: Mike Wazowski!
parenthetical: (Boo gives Mike a big hug. He tenses, then relaxes and hugs her back.)

speaker: Mike
text: Ah, Boo, it's been fun.
parenthetical: (Boo waves bye-bye to Mike as she turns back to Sulley.)

speaker: Mike
text: Go ahead. Go grow up.
parenthetical: (Sulley smiles down at Boo. Ready, Sulley opens her door. Boo squeals with delight. Boo runs into the room, happy to be home. She pulls Sulley into her room and hands him various toys, each one with a new sense of excitement.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Uh... Boo... um... Oh, look at that. Yeah, you know-- Oh, that's cute. Yeah... uh... Boo...
parenthetical: (Boo hands him a teddy bear, Jessie, the Luxo Jr. ball, and a squeaky clown fish.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Well, that's very nice.
parenthetical: (Sulley realizes it's up to him to say goodbye. He grabs her playfully and flies her like an airplane to her bed.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Come here, you!
parenthetical: (Boo giggles wildly as Sulley deposits her in bed. he tucks the teddy bear in next to her.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Oh, he's a happy bear...
parenthetical: (Boo looks to her closet and points.)

speaker: Boo
text: Dars byargs lub.
parenthetical: [gibberish]

speaker: Sulley
text: Nothing's coming out of the closet to scare you anymore. Right?
parenthetical: (Boo smiles and gives a little nod. Sulley tickles her chin.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Yeah. Good-bye, Boo.

speaker: Boo
text: Kitty.

speaker: Sulley
text: Kitty has to go.
parenthetical: (Boo gives Sulley a big hug and Sulley hugs her back. Sulley sets her back in bed, then walks slowly to the door. He looks back at her one last time. Boo watches him sadly from her bed. Sulley turns away, slowly closing the closet door after him. Boo climbs down from her bed. She runs to her closet and opens it.)

speaker: Boo
text: Boo!
parenthetical: (Boo jumps in front of the open door, but only sees her closet with her stuff in it)

speaker: Boo
text: Kitty?
parenthetical: (The portal to the monster world has closed. In the simulator room, Boo's door is shredded.)

speaker: Roz
text: None of this ever happened, gentlemen. And I don't wanna see any paperwork on this.
parenthetical: (Roz and the CDA Agents exit. Sulley and Mike share a silent moment. Mike notices a single piece of Boo's door on the floor. He hands it to Sulley. Sulley clasps the small chip in his large hand. Outside the factory, The CDA agents shut Waternoose into the back of the van and drive away, a la Clockwork Orange. A large crowd of M.I. employees are mad, upset and disappointed over his betrayal. They murmur with concern about what will happen now.)

speaker: CDA agent
text: Take him away.
gender_rationale: grouped numbered CDA agents (CDA agent #N); individual agents not distinguished, gender not stated

speaker: Smitty
text: I bet we get the rest of the day off.

speaker: Needleman
text: YOU IDIOT! They're gonna shut down the factory!
parenthetical: (Everyone gasps in shock, worrying about this. Sulley and Mike walk out into the evening light.)

speaker: Mike
text: I'm tellin' ya, pal, when that wall went up, you should've seen the look on Waternoose's face. Hoo-hoo! I hope we get a copy of that tape.
parenthetical: (Mike looks up at Sulley. His buddy looks pretty sad.)

speaker: Mike
text: Hey, you alright? Come on, pal, cheer up. We did it! We got Boo home. Ah, sure we put the factory in the toilet, and... gee, hundreds of people will be outta work now. Not to mention the angry mob that'll come after us when there's no more power, but hey! At least we had some laughs, right?
parenthetical: (As Mike continues walking, Sulley slowly comes to a stop, an idea forming in his mind.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Laughs.

speaker: Mike
text: Hey, is this thing on? Hello, hello. Testing, testing.
parenthetical: (The kid turns on a light, revealing Mike, sitting on a stool, microphone in hand.)

speaker: Mike
text: Hey, good evening, how are you? Nice to see you. I'll tell you, it's great to be here in... your room. Where are you from?
parenthetical: (The kid stares at Mike, confused.)

speaker: Mike
text: Never mind. You're in kindergarten, right? Oh, I love kindergarten. Best three years of my life.
parenthetical: (No response. Is that supposed to be funny?)

speaker: Mike
text: Of my life. But I love sports. Dodge-ball was the best. Oh, yeah, I was the fastest one out there. Of course I was the ball...
parenthetical: (The kid looks on, skeptically. That was worse than the last joke. Mike knows he's dying.)

speaker: Mike
text: ...but I... was the ball... see? All right.
parenthetical: (Mike stands up, tosses the microphone in his mouth and swallows it. Amplified stomach gurgles are heard while he waits. He finally lets out a huge burp, projecting the microphone out of his mouth and catching it.)

speaker: Mike
text: Huh?
parenthetical: (The kid howls with laughter. Mike grabs the stool and heads for the closet door.)

speaker: Mike
text: Hey, thanks a lot! I'll be here all week. Remember to tip your waitresses.
parenthetical: (The Scare Floor has now officially become The Laugh Floor. Mike smiles as a gigantic can fills with laughs.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Great job, Mikey. You filled your quota on the first kid of the day.

speaker: Mike
text: Not bad, huh? You know, only somebody with perfect comedic timing could produce this much energy in one shot.

speaker: Sulley
text: Uh-huh. And the fact that laughter is ten times more powerful than scream had nothing to do with it?
parenthetical: (Mike clears his throat, sheepishly.)

speaker: Celia
text: Oh, Googly Bear... Come here, you.
parenthetical: (Celia calls to Mike from a nearby desk. Mike tosses the microphone and runs to her.)

speaker: Mike
text: Schmoopsie-Poo!

speaker: Celia
text: Googly...
parenthetical: (Celia kisses Mike on his mouth, and he feels loved by that. Celia's snakes begin kissing Mike, too, and it tickles him.)

speaker: Celia
text: Girls, girls! Ple- stop, stop, stop. Michael, you're such a charmer.

speaker: Mike
text: Hey, did you bring the magazine?

speaker: Celia
text: They just delivered a whole box.

speaker: Mike
text: Let me see it! Ha ha ha! Sulley and I made the cover, right?
parenthetical: (He opens the box and we see: "BUSINESS SHRIEK" magazine. The lead article, "MONSTERS, INC. BACK ON TOP!" shows Sulley in full view. Mike's face is totally obscured by the bar code.)

speaker: Mike
text: I don't believe it.

speaker: Celia
text: Googly Bear.
parenthetical: (Mike lifts up a copy of the magazine and holds it in front of him.)

speaker: Mike
text: I'm on the cover of a maga-zine! OW!!
parenthetical: (Peterson inserts chattering teeth into his mouth as he walks into a closet door. Fungus puts on a pair of three-eyed grouch glasses.)

speaker: Fungus
text: Oh, this is great!
parenthetical: (George walks by carrying a watermelon and a mallet, familiar to Dr. Seuss. Bile backs out of a door, jacks sticking from his butt. Laughter emanates from the kid's room, filling the tank. Satisfied, Bile bounces a soccer ball and smiles. Sulley makes his way to the end of the hall. He surveys the fruit of his labors. Laughter emanates from all the doors. Workers happily move giant scream cans, tell each other jokes, juggle and enjoy themselves. A graph on Sulley's clipboard shows first year profits going through the roof. But Sulley's smile fades. taped to the clipboard backing is the picture Boo drew of herself and Sulley. Below is the wooden chip from her door.)

speaker: Mike
text: Hey, Sulley.
parenthetical: (Sulley covers the picture as Mike approaches.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Ah! Hey, uh, Mike. I was, uh...

speaker: Mike
text: Well, listen, if you got a minute, there's something I wanna show you. Okay? Close your eyes, follow me.

speaker: Mike
text: Come on. No peeking!
parenthetical: (Sulley follows Mike with a sigh.)

speaker: Mike
text: Keep coming, keep coming, keep coming. Come on, keep coming, keep coming...

speaker: Sulley
text: Mike...

speaker: Mike
text: Come on, and follow the sultry sound of my voice. Okay, stop. Open 'em.
parenthetical: (Sulley opens his eyes. Before him sits Boo's reconstructed door, meticulously glued back together.)

speaker: Mike
text: Ta-dah!

speaker: Sulley
text: Mike! Is that...?

speaker: Mike
text: Sorry it took so long, pal. There was a lot of wood to go through. You know, it only works if you have every piece.
parenthetical: (The door is clearly missing a piece. Sulley looks down to his clipboard where the sliver of Boo's door is taped. Sulley fits the piece into the door. It fits perfectly. The red light above the door glows. Mike smiles and steps aside. Sulley reaches for the door-knob. The door opens. Sulley's nervous face is hit with light as he scans the room.)

speaker: Sulley
text: Boo?

speaker: Boo
text: Kitty!
parenthetical: (Sulley smiles.)
